> If the player's background is ordinary citizen (roll 7), they must consult with the game master to determine one general skill appropriate for the character and acquire it at 3rd level. At this time, both the game master and the player should clearly know what the chosen general skill can do (the abilities that can be used with that general skill).

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00308))_

> If the player's background is merchant/scholar (roll 8), they must choose either 3rd level in the merchant skill or 3rd level in the sage skill for their character to acquire.

## For Races Other Than Humans

- For races other than human (except for half-elves raised by humans), the starting skills and experience points are determined by race (Table 1-6: Starting Experience by Race). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00311))_
- For races other than human (except for half-elves raised by humans), the starting skills and experience points are determined by race (Table 1-6: Starting Experience by Race). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00311))_

## Example:

- Since the character we're creating is a human, we'll use the backgrounds table. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00313))_
- The character ended up coming from the mercenary background. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00313))_
- Therefore, we'll have 1 level in the fighter skill as our starting skill, and 2500 starting experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00313))_
- Therefore, we'll have 1 level in the fighter skill as our starting skill, and 2500 starting experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00313))_
- In addition, money is listed as 2Dx200. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00314))_
- The result of our next 2D roll is 6 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00314))_
- This means we roll 2D and have money (in gamels ) equal to the result times 200. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00314))_
- The character has 1200 gamels. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00314))_
- This means we roll 2D and have money (in gamels ) equal to the result times 200. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00314))_

## 1.4.4 Age, Gender, Name

- A character must be given a name, and their age and gender must be decided. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00316))_
- These things may be decided at any stage of character creation. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00316))_
- You can decide at the beginning. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00316))_
- This is because the part of the process that uses random numbers for character creation has just been completed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00317))_
- In practice, however, this is a good time to decide. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00317))_
- This is because the part of the process that uses random numbers for character creation has just been completed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00317))_
- Of course, no matter what name you choose, it will not affect their abilities. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00318))_
- In Sword World RPG , these are not related to the numeric aspects of the game. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00318))_
- This should make it easier to decide on the age, gender, and name of the character. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00318))_
- In other words, it is assumed that there are no differences or changes in ability based on gender or age. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00318))_
- Conversely, once the ability scores are created and the character's initial experience is determined, you can usually get an impression of the character. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00318))_

## 《 Age 》

- It is basically impossible to play as a child who has not fully grown both physically and mentally, or an old man who is clearly on the physical decline. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00320))_
- In addition, in Sword World RPG , newly created characters are green youths , so to speak, who don't have much experience. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00320))_
- When deciding on the age of a character, it should be between 15 years old and around 30 years old at most for a human. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00321))_
- In Alecrast, the average life span of a human is about 50 years due to underdeveloped medical care. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00321))_
- Of course, it's natural that they'll die earlier than this if they're ill or wounded. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00321))_
- The life span of a human is about 100 years old. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00321))_

## 《 Ages of Each Race 》

- There was a time when the socially accepted age of adulthood was about 15 years old, and even younger. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00324))_
- However, it is one thing to be physically mature and another to be socially recognized as an adult. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00324))_
- In Japan, where we live, we are considered adults at the age of 20, but there are some actions that are recognized as adult at the age of 18. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00324))_
- Whether or not a person is recognized as an adult is not only a matter of physical growth, but also a matter of social judgment. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00324))_
- Whether or not a person is recognized as an adult is not only a matter of physical growth, but also a matter of social judgment. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00324))_
- In the human societies of Alecrast , 15 years old is considered to be the age of adulthood. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00325))_
- However, in the societies of other races, you basically have to be older to be considered an adult, and the longer the life span of the race, the later they tend to do so. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00325))_
- As a result, the time for characters of other races to be recognized as adults and have the opportunity to go on adventures will be significantly later than it is for humans; around 30 years of age for dwarves, 40 years for grassrunners, and 100 for elves. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00326))_
- This is because it' s considered common sense in human society. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00327))_
- Half-elves, if raised in human society, are treated as adults at around age 15, just like humans. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00327))_
- However, because they are clearly known to have a shorter lifespan than elves and because they are essentially detested, they are not made to wait until they are 100 years old and are forced out of society at around age 30. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00327))_
- However, because they are clearly known to have a shorter lifespan than elves and because they are essentially detested, they are not made to wait until they are 100 years old and are forced out of society at around age 30. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00327))_
- This is because it' s considered common sense in human society. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00327))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00323))_

> For races other than humans, consider age as follows. Even for different races with long life spans, the rate of growth to adulthood is not much different from that of humans. Therefore, once they reach around 20 years of age, they are physically fit enough to embark on an adventure.

## 《 Gender 》

- Therefore, the choice of gender is completely free. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00330))_
- As mentioned before, Sword World RPG treats men and women as having no difference in ability. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00330))_
- Therefore, the choice of gender is completely free. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00330))_
- As mentioned before, Sword World RPG treats men and women as having no difference in ability. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00330))_

> Even races other than humans have two genders, male and female, exactly the same as humans. Also consider the differences in appearance between men and women, as well as the differences in the division of roles in society, to be equivalent to those of humans.

## 《 Name 》

- Naturally, a character must have a name. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00333))_
- It's not convenient to enjoy the fictional world of Forcelia if they're always called by the player's name or just hey.... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00333))_
- Therefore, a character should be given a name that is appropriate for Forcelia. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00334))_
- Therefore, a character should be given a name that is appropriate for Forcelia. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00334))_
- After all, the names of most people in Alecrast , the setting of Sword World RPG , are like that. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00335))_
- Purely Japanese or Chinese names are not suitable. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00335))_
- When deciding on a name for a character, it is usually best to stick with a basic western-style name. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00335))_
- After all, the names of most people in Alecrast , the setting of Sword World RPG , are like that. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00335))_
- In rare cases, a middle name may also be added between the first and last names. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00336))_
- In Alecrast , first name, then last name are usually written in that order. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00336))_
- Alternatively, it's acceptable to simply decide on a first name and leave the last unspecified, or to use a nickname or alias, as is often used by thieves. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00336))_
- In Alecrast , first name, then last name are usually written in that order. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00336))_

## Example:

- This character comes from the mercenary background, so being too young is not an option. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00339))_
- Since they're a human, their age will be around 15~30 years old. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00339))_
- Although there are many women who fight in Forcelia, mercenaries will probably still have a higher percentage of men. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00340))_
- Either gender is acceptable. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00340))_
- In this section, we'll use Ducard Sample Ⅱ (the second), though it's not a very sensible name. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00341))_
- Y ou may follow your own sensibilities, or you may borrow the name of a real westerner. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00341))_

## 1.4.5 Character Growth via Starting Experience Points

- In addition to this, starting experience points can also be used to acquire new skills or increase a character's skills. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00343))_
- A character has already acquired certain skills (starting skills) according to Table 1-5: Backgrounds or Table 1-6: Starting Experience by Race. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00343))_
- However, the skills that can be acquired or increased are limited to those of adventurer skills, and general skills cannot be acquired or increased. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00344))_
- However, the skills that can be acquired or increased are limited to those of adventurer skills, and general skills cannot be acquired or increased. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00344))_
- For example, if you want to acquire 1 level in ranger, you can do so by subtracting 500 from your starting experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00346))_
- For example, if you want to acquire 1 level in ranger, you can do so by subtracting 500 from your starting experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00346))_
- This should be understood as purchasing skills with experience points . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00347))_
- Note, however, that experience points are spent at each level attained. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00348))_
- It is also possible to increase the level of skills that have already been acquired. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00348))_
- You may even acquire 2 or more levels at a time, so long as there are enough experience points to spend. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00348))_
- General skills cannot be acquired or increased with experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00350))_
- Only adventurer skills can be acquired or increased using experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00350))_
- Only adventurer skills can be acquired or increased using experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00350))_
- In addition, starting experience points do not have to be used up. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00351))_
- Leaving them in place will give you an advantage in the growth of your character after the game. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00351))_
- Leaving them in place will give you an advantage in the growth of your character after the game. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00351))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00345))_

> The experience points required to increase a skill are shown in Table 1-7: Experience Points by Skill (at Character Creation).

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00345))_

> The experience points required to increase a skill are shown in Table 1-7: Experience Points by Skill (at Character Creation).

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00346))_

> Characters can spend their starting experience points to gain skill levels.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00346))_

> Characters can spend their starting experience points to gain skill levels. For example, if you want to acquire 1 level in ranger, you can do so by subtracting 500 from your starting experience points.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00348))_

> For example, if you do not have the ranger skill and you want to acquire 2 levels in ranger at once, you must spend 500 + 1000 for a total of 1500 experience points.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00348))_

> It is also possible to increase the level of skills that have already been acquired. You may even acquire 2 or more levels at a time, so long as there are enough experience points to spend. Note, however, that experience points are spent at each level attained. For example, if you do not have the ranger skill and you want to acquire 2 levels in ranger at once, you must spend 500 + 1000 for a total of 1500 experience points.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00349))_

> If you have enough experience points, you can acquire multiple skills.

- With these, for example, we could acquire 1 level in sorcerer (25002000= 500 remaining) or increase fighter to 2 levels (2500-1500= 1000 remaining). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00353))_
- Ducard II has 2500 experience points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00353))_

- This skill is closely related to the sage skill and requires fewer experience points when acquiring both. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00356))_
- You may acquire sorcerer only, but it's better to avoid this, as it will put you at a disadvantage during the game. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00356))_

- Of course, having the sage skill does not mean that you're required to have the sorcerer skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00357))_
- It is important to note that it is against the rules for a character who has neither the sorcerer nor sage skills to acquire only the sorcerer skill for 1500 points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00358))_
- It is important to note that it is against the rules for a character who has neither the sorcerer nor sage skills to acquire only the sorcerer skill for 1500 points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00358))_

## Required Strength

- Even swords of the same shape are sold in a variety of sizes to match the physical strength of the person using it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00368))_
- For an adventurer, the first step to survival is to choose a weapon most suited to their strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00369))_
- Therefore, it's a good idea to choose one based on the image of your character. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00370))_
- As long as you choose that, you are guaranteed the same attack capability, even if you use a different type of weapon. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00370))_
- Basically, the most suitable weapon is one whose required strength equals the character's strength . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00370))_
- Think of a sword, ax, or spear with a required strength of 14 as the same in terms of game value. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00370))_
- Therefore, it's a good idea to choose one based on the image of your character. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00370))_
- However, most weapons have a limited range of required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00371))_
- However, most weapons have a limited range of required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00371))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00370))_

> Basically, the most suitable weapon is one whose required strength equals the character's strength . As long as you choose that, you are guaranteed the same attack capability, even if you use a different type of weapon. Think of a sword, ax, or spear with a required strength of 14 as the same in terms of game value. Therefore, it's a good idea to choose one based on the image of your character.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00368))_

> When buying weapons and armor, one must be aware of their required strength .

## One-Handed and TwoHanded Weapons

- A two-handed weapon will deal more damage to an opponent than a onehanded weapon of the same required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00373))_
- 1H stands for one-handed . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00374))_
- 2H stands for two-handed . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00374))_
- To determine whether a weapon is one-handed or two-handed, see the usage column on Table 1-8: Weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00374))_
- 1~2H means the weapon can be wielded with one or both hands. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00374))_
- 1~2H means the weapon can be wielded with one or both hands. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00374))_

## Projectiles

- Note that these cannot be used in melee. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00376))_
- On Table 1-8: Weapons, weapons with made for throwing or shooting after the weapon's name are strictly projectile weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00376))_
- On Table 1-8: Weapons, weapons with made for throwing or shooting after the weapon's name are strictly projectile weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00376))_

## Armor

- The principle of choose one whose required strength equals the character's strength applies to armor as well. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00379))_
- There are several types, but choose which one you think is appropriate (Table 1-9: Armor). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00379))_

## Shields

- In the case of shields, there are only two types: small shields (required strength 1) and large shields (required strength 13). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00381))_
- Therefore, it is safe to choose the less expensive small shield (Table 1-10: Shields). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00381))_
- However, in terms of increasing a character's defensive ability, the effects of small and large shields are basically the same in this game. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00381))_
- Therefore, it is safe to choose the less expensive small shield (Table 1-10: Shields). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00381))_
- In the case of shields, there are only two types: small shields (required strength 1) and large shields (required strength 13). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00381))_

## Price Calculation

- There you will find expressions like x20+40 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00383))_
- Weapons and armor have different prices for the same shape, depending on the required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00383))_
- The required strength is applied to this formula to find the actual price. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00383))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00383))_

> Weapons and armor have different prices for the same shape, depending on the required strength. For the prices of weapons and armor, please see the price calculation column of the weapons table. There you will find expressions like x20+40 . The required strength is applied to this formula to find the actual price. For example, for a broadsword with a required strength of 14, the price would be:

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00384))_

> 14 (required strength) x 20 + 40 = 320. Prices are in gamels ( G ).

## 1.4.7 Restrictions on Weapons and Armor Based on Skill

- In this case, it wouldn't make sense to have the shaman skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00386))_
- The general rule is to choose one whose required strength equals the character's strength , but a character may be substantially limited by the skills they have. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00386))_
- For example, if one with the shaman skill wears metal armor, they cannot use spirit magic, which is the primary ability of the shaman skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00386))_
- For example, if one with the shaman skill wears metal armor, they cannot use spirit magic, which is the primary ability of the shaman skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00386))_
- The general rule is to choose one whose required strength equals the character's strength , but a character may be substantially limited by the skills they have. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00386))_
- For characters with multiple skills, look at the description of each skill and decide which weapons and armor you will buy. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00387))_
- The following are guidelines for purchasing weapons and armor based on which skills a character has. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00387))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00386))_

> The general rule is to choose one whose required strength equals the character's strength , but a character may be substantially limited by the skills they have. For example, if one with the shaman skill wears metal armor, they cannot use spirit magic, which is the primary ability of the shaman skill. In this case, it wouldn't make sense to have the shaman skill.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00387))_

> If the equipment has both unrestricted and restricted skills, please understand that your equipment must conform to the restricted skill if you want to take full advantage of both skills.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00387))_

> The following are guidelines for purchasing weapons and armor based on which skills a character has. For characters with multiple skills, look at the description of each skill and decide which weapons and armor you will buy. If the equipment has both unrestricted and restricted skills, please understand that your equipment must conform to the restricted skill if you want to take full advantage of both skills.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00389))_

> Table 1-10: Shields

## [ Fighter ］

- The basic idea is to choose weapons and armor whose required strength is the same as your own strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00391))_
- Weapons and armor that have a required strength less than your own strength will make your character's attack and defense incomplete, and weapons and armor with a required strength greater than your own strength cannot be handled. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00391))_

- Specifically, weapons and armor that require up to half your strength (rounded up) . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00393))_

## [ Shaman ]

- If you want to make use of your shaman skill, your armor is limited to cloth , soft leather , or hard leather . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00403))_
- Of these, the one with the greatest required strength within the character's strength range should be chosen. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00403))_
- Also, you should not buy a shield. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00403))_
- Of these, the one with the greatest required strength within the character's strength range should be chosen. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00403))_
- If you want to make use of your shaman skill, your armor is limited to cloth , soft leather , or hard leather . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00403))_
- It is fundamental to match your strength to the required strength of the weapon. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00404))_
- When choosing a weapon, since you're not buying a shield, it's a good idea to choose a two-handed weapon if at all possible. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00404))_

## [ Weight Limits ]

- However, Sword World RPG does not do this. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00421))_
- Many games set some kind of weight on weapons, armor and equipment, limiting the amount of goods a character can carry. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00421))_
- Instead, please use your common sense to make decisions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00421))_
- Many games set some kind of weight on weapons, armor and equipment, limiting the amount of goods a character can carry. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00421))_
- A character cannot have an infinite number of things. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00422))_
- This would be a matter of course even if the rules do not stipulate it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00422))_
- It is impossible to hold 1000 torches or 1 kilometer of rope. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00422))_

## Example:

- Ducard II has 580 gamels left. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00424))_
- Since we're going on an adventure, a water bag (20G) is a must. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00425))_
- Without it, it'll be difficult to carry luggage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00425))_
- First, we'll need a rucksack (50G). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00425))_
- We should also have a belt pouch (15G) for small items and money. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00425))_
- Tableware (12G) and cooking utensils (50G) will also be needed for camping. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00426))_
- A tent is too expensive and we don't need something large enough for five people. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00426))_
- If he has friends, though, it would be nice if we could pool our money together to buy it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00426))_
- If we're planning to sleep outdoors, we'll need a mantle (40G) and a blanket (50G). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00426))_
- A lantern would be brighter, but money is tight and we can't afford that luxury. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00427))_
- Without that, a fire cannot be started. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00427))_
- We'll also need torches (6 for 5 G). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00427))_
- For a lighting tool, we'll first need a tinderbox (20G). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00427))_
- It may be useful at some point. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00429))_
- Ducard II's ranger skill includes the map creation ability. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00429))_
- The thieves' tools are for a thief and the musical instruments are for a bard, so they're not necessary for Ducard II. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00429))_
- So far, 277 gamels have been spent on purchases, with 303 gamels remaining. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00430))_
- We won't buy preserved food yet. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00431))_

## 1.4.9

## Filling Out the Character Sheet

- The Character Sheet that accompanies this book is designed to record and list various character details. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00436))_
- This will help keep the game running smoothly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00436))_

## [ ① Player Name ]

- The player should write their own name in the player name field. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00440))_

## [ ② Race ]

- Once you've decided, please write it here. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00442))_
- The first decision in character creation is race. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00442))_

## [ ③ Sub-Ability Scores]

- Once race is chosen, sub-ability scores can be determined. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00444))_

## [ ④ Ability Scores ]

- Once sub-ability scores are determined, ability scores are calculated by addition. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00446))_

## [ ⑤ Ability Bonuses ]

- A bonus is an ability score divided by 6 (rounded down). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00448))_
- Once ability scores have been calculated, the next step is the ability bonuses. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00448))_

## [ ⑥ Origin ]

- If the character was not raised in a human society, there is no need to write anything in this field. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00450))_
- Once it's been decided, write it in the origin field. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00450))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00450))_

> If the character is a human or a halfelf raised by a human, Table 1-5: Backgrounds must be used to determine their origin.

## [ ⑦ Starting Experience Points ]

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00452))_

> Please write down starting experience points as determined by Table 1-5: Backgrounds or Table 1-6: Starting Experience by Race in the experience points field.

## [ ⑧ Starting Skills

## [ ⑨ Money ]

- Table 1-5: Backgrounds or Table 1-6: Starting Experience by Race determine the amount of money (2D x modifier ) that you have. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00457))_

## [ ⑩ Gender ]

- It should now be easier to decide on gender and age, plus the character's name. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00459))_
- Once the process has been completed up to this point, you should have developed an image of the character. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00459))_

## [ ⑪ Age ]

## [ ⑫ Character Name ]

## [ ⑬ Increase Skills ]

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00466))_

> Starting experience points can be used to acquire new adventurer skills or to increase any skills already acquired (Table 1-7: Experience Points by Skill).

## [ ⑭ Adventurer Level ]

- Once you've acquired your final skills (starting skill and growth via starting experience points), you can determine your adventurer level. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00469))_
- Adventurer level is equal to the highest adventurer skill level. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00470))_

## [ ⑮ Purchase Equipment]

- Write down the items you purchased then rewrite your money, reducing by the amount of money you spent (Table 1-11: Equipment). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00472))_
- Write down the items you purchased then rewrite your money, reducing by the amount of money you spent (Table 1-11: Equipment). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00472))_

> Use your money to purchase equipment. Write down the items you purchased then rewrite your money, reducing by the amount of money you spent (Table 1-11: Equipment).

> When you purchase weapons, shields, and armor, write them in the appropriate fields (Table 1-8: Weapons, Table 1-9: Armor, and Table 1-10: Shields).

## 2.1

## Baseline Scores

- A baseline score is a number that indicates how competent or skilled a character is at a given activity. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00487))_
- skill level , and their dexterity bonus is used for ability bonus . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00488))_
- Also, if the character is trained or experienced as a warrior, they'll be even more likely to hit their target. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00489))_
- For example, if you want to hit an enemy with your sword, it is important to have dexterity to wield it accurately. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00489))_
- For example, if you want to hit an enemy with your sword, it is important to have dexterity to wield it accurately. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00489))_
- In Sword World RPG , this is expressed as relevant skill level + ability bonus and is called the baseline score . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00490))_
- In Sword World RPG , this is expressed as relevant skill level + ability bonus and is called the baseline score . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00490))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00489))_

> For example, if you want to hit an enemy with your sword, it is important to have dexterity to wield it accurately. Also, if the character is trained or experienced as a warrior, they'll be even more likely to hit their target.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00491))_

> baseline score = relevant skill level + ability bonus

## Example:

- His fighter skill level is 2 and his dexterity bonus is +2. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00495))_
- Fighter is only useful in combat. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00499))_
- The sorcerer, shaman, and priest rune master skills are also of little use for anything other than casting magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00499))_
- Fighter is only useful in combat. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00499))_
- Other skills are used in a variety of situations. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00500))_
- Once the baseline score and target score are determined, a success roll check can finally be made. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00502))_
- The result is called the fi nal score . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00503))_
- This final score should be equal to or greater than the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00503))_
- The result is called the fi nal score . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00503))_

> 2+2= 4

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00502))_

> Once the baseline score and target score are determined, a success roll check can finally be made.

> The player must roll two dice and add their baseline score to the roll.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00503))_

> The player must roll two dice and add their baseline score to the roll. The result is called the fi nal score . This final score should be equal to or greater than the target score.

> final score (baseline score + 2D) ≥ target score → action is successful

## 2.3.1 Automatic Success and Automatic Failure

- Even the most difficult acts can succeed by a quirk of fate. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00522))_
- This is represented by the automatic success and automatic failure rules. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00522))_
- On the other hand, no matter how simple something may seem, it may fail due to some misfortune. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00522))_
- On a success roll, if a player rolls a 12 , i.e., both dice roll 6 ( double sixes ), the action is a success , no matter how low the baseline score nor how high the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00523))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00523))_

> On a success roll, if a player rolls a 12 , i.e., both dice roll 6 ( double sixes ), the action is a success , no matter how low the baseline score nor how high the target score.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00524))_

> Conversely, if that player rolls a 2 , that is, if the two dice both roll a 1 ( double ones ), the action fails , no matter

## 2.4

- It simply means that you succeeded or failed, not that you succeeded very well or failed very badly . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00527))_
- Basically, automatic success and automatic failure are not definitive success or definitive failure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00527))_
- It simply means that you succeeded or failed, not that you succeeded very well or failed very badly . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00527))_

## Bonuses and Penalties

- A success roll may be subject to modifiers. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00530))_
- This is to replicate any favorable or unfavorable circumstances when a character is about to perform an action. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00530))_
- For example, if the person you're fighting is falling, their movements will be restricted and your attacks will be easier to land. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00531))_
- For example, if the person you're fighting is falling, their movements will be restricted and your attacks will be easier to land. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00531))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00530))_

> A success roll may be subject to modifiers. This is to replicate any favorable or unfavorable circumstances when a character is about to perform an action.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00531))_

> On the other hand, if you're fighting in the dark, then unless your character has darkvision, you cannot see your opponent, so it is not so easy to hit him.

## 2.5

- In such cases, the game master applies a bonus or penalty to the success roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00533))_
- When such bonuses or penalties are applied, the baseline score will go up or down. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00534))_
- A player must also calculate these before making a success roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00534))_
- A player must also calculate these before making a success roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00534))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00534))_

> When such bonuses or penalties are applied, the baseline score will go up or down. A player must also calculate these before making a success roll.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00535))_

> final score {baseline score + 2D ± (bonus, penalty)} ≥ target score → action is successful

## 2.5.1

## Difficulty

- Difficulty is a score determined by the game master based on the difficulty of the actions the character attempts. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00559))_
- What makes this different from a target score is that this is not the direct number the player is aiming for. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00559))_

## 2.5.2 How to Make a Difficulty Check

- Whether the base difficulty is revealed or not depends on the situation. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00561))_
- This roll is then added to the difficulty and used as the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00561))_
- To make a difficulty check , the game master also rolls the dice (2D). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00561))_
- The game master does not reveal their roll nor the target score that results from their calculation, to the players. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00561))_
- This roll is then added to the difficulty and used as the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00561))_
- The players' dice rolls are otherwise the same. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00562))_
- They will be told the baseline score, roll 2D, and modify it with any bonuses or penalties to find a final score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00562))_
- The game master then takes the final score and compares it to the target score (difficulty + 2D) that they have secretly set. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00563))_
- If there is a tie or if the final score is greater, the action is a success ; if it is less, the action is a failure . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00563))_
- The game master then takes the final score and compares it to the target score (difficulty + 2D) that they have secretly set. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00563))_
- The game master does not say whether the result is the action succeeded or the action failed , because a difficulty check is made when the success or failure of the action is not known to the characters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00566))_
- They must only answer as much as the character can tell from their successes and failures. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00566))_
- The game master does not say whether the result is the action succeeded or the action failed , because a difficulty check is made when the success or failure of the action is not known to the characters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00566))_
- They must only answer as much as the character can tell from their successes and failures. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00566))_
- In the case of #2, if you assume there are no traps, you will have a terrible time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00568))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00562, source-range-de1806a5-00566))_

> The players' dice rolls are otherwise the same. They will be told the baseline score, roll 2D, and modify it with any bonuses or penalties to find a final score. The game master does not say whether the result is the action succeeded or the action failed , because a difficulty check is made when the success or failure of the action is not known to the characters. They must only answer as much as the character can tell from their successes and failures. Let's show how the game master's answer wou

> final score ≥ target score (difficulty + 2D) → action succeeds

## 2.5.3 Analogy of Success and Failure

- If a success roll is made for a difficulty check, the success or failure of the action is strictly unknown to the player. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00570))_
- That is precisely the reason why we use difficulty checks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00570))_
- However, a player is given only one criteria to determine whether the success roll they made for a difficulty check is a success or a failure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00571))_
- However, a player is given only one criteria to determine whether the success roll they made for a difficulty check is a success or a failure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00571))_
- That is: the dice that the players themselves have rolled. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00572))_
- Therefore, if after examining the door, you do not find a trap, the risk is quite low even if you assume that there really are no traps. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00573))_
- Therefore, if after examining the door, you do not find a trap, the risk is quite low even if you assume that there really are no traps. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00573))_
- So, even if you examine the door and do not find any traps, you should be skeptical of the results. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00574))_
- In particular, when a player rolls either of the two extremes, double sixes or double ones , it means automatic success or automatic failure , so that the player can clearly see whether the success roll is successful or not. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00575))_
- In particular, when a player rolls either of the two extremes, double sixes or double ones , it means automatic success or automatic failure , so that the player can clearly see whether the success roll is successful or not. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00575))_
- A player's dice roll should be interpreted as an expression of confidence in the character's actions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00576))_
- A high roll indicates a high level of confidence, while a low roll indicates a lack of confidence in what they've done. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00576))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00571))_

> However, a player is given only one criteria to determine whether the success roll they made for a difficulty check is a success or a failure.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00573))_

> If the roll is as high as 10 or 11, the success roll is considered to have a very high chance of success.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00573))_

> If the roll is as high as 10 or 11, the success roll is considered to have a very high chance of success. Therefore, if after examining the door, you do not find a trap, the risk is quite low even if you assume that there really are no traps.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00574))_

> If the roll is low, such as 3 or 4, then there's a high chance that the success roll has failed.

## Caution

- It has been noted before that double sixes and double ones are automatic successes or failures and do not imply definitive successes or failures. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00578))_
- It has been noted before that double sixes and double ones are automatic successes or failures and do not imply definitive successes or failures. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00578))_
- When the player hears this, if they reply like the following, it's a prime example of this misinterpretation: Oh, I screwed up big time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00579))_
- Which means I can say for sure 'There are definitely no traps on this door'. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00579))_
- It is especially bad to misinterpret an automatic failure as a definitive failure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00579))_
- For example, if a player is trying to find a trap on a door and has rolled double ones, the game master will of course tell the player you didn't find any traps . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00579))_
- Which means I can say for sure 'There are definitely no traps on this door'. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00579))_
- For example, if a player is trying to find a trap on a door and has rolled double ones, the game master will of course tell the player you didn't find any traps . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00579))_
- Consider it like I never checked the door. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00581))_

## 2.6

## Success Rolls Using Adventurer Level

- For a success roll, the baseline score is usually calculated as relevant skill level + ability bonus . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00586))_
- However, in some cases, adventurer level , rather than skill level, is used to calculate the baseline score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00586))_
- adventuring, the baseline score for a success roll is adventurer level + ability bonus . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00589))_
- Actions that fit into this category are explained in 6.6: Common Adventurer Abilities . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00590))_

## Resistance Rolls

- Similarly, a success roll made using adventurer level + mental power bonus as the baseline score is specifically called a mental power resistance roll , and the two are collectively called simply resistance rolls . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00593))_
- A success roll made using adventurer level + life force bonus as the baseline score is specifically referred to as a life force resistance roll . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00593))_
- The reason for the name change and the distinction between the two in particular, is that they are frequently performed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00594))_
- Therefore, these baseline scores are also defined by the terms life force resistance and mental power resistance , among others. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00595))_
- Therefore, these baseline scores are also defined by the terms life force resistance and mental power resistance , among others. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00595))_
- Life force resistance rolls are made to avoid or reduce the effects of physical dangers such as poison, while mental power resistance rolls are made to avoid or reduce the effects of magic or curses. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00596))_
- Life force resistance rolls are made to avoid or reduce the effects of physical dangers such as poison, while mental power resistance rolls are made to avoid or reduce the effects of magic or curses. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00596))_
- Checks against special attacks by monsters that are magical in nature are also made with mental power resistance rolls. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00597))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00596))_

> Life force resistance rolls are made to avoid or reduce the effects of physical dangers such as poison, while mental power resistance rolls are made to avoid or reduce the effects of magic or curses.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00597))_

> In particular, all checks against magic, even if it causes physical damage, are made with mental power resistance rolls.

## Example 1:

- Paralyzing poison is a special ability of ghouls. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00599))_
- The toxicity score of ghoul poison is 10, which is the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00599))_
- During the adventure, he is attacked and wounded by a ghoul. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00599))_
- Since Ducard II has an adventurer level of 2 and a +3 life force bonus, the baseline score of his life force resistance roll (life force resistance power) is 5 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00599))_
- Our 2D roll is 5, so with 5+5= 10 , he escapes paralysis, if barely. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00599))_

## Example 2:

- Since Ducard II has an adventurer level of 2 and a +2 mental power bonus, the baseline score of his mental power resistance roll (mental power resistance power) is 4 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00601))_
- While touring an underground cave on an adventure, he wanders into a mysterious room where all four walls are painted in extreme colors. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00601))_
- In fact, there is a magic trap set in this room that will put anyone who enters it into a deep sleep. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00602))_
- The richly colored patterns in the room begin to move strangely, and Ducard II is struck by a strong sense of drowsiness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00602))_
- Normally, this would never succeed, but our 2D roll is 12 ! _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00602))_

## Ability Scores

- When a character with adventurer level 0 (a character with no adventurer skills) makes these checks, the baseline score is 0 regardless of their ability scores. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00606))_

## Chapter 3:

## Action Announcements

## 3.1.1 Order of Action Announcements

- Each round begins with an announcement of what your character intends to do. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00619))_
- In combat, there are basically two opposing groups. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00620))_
- Suppose there are three characters, a1, a2, and a3 in group A and two characters, b1 and b2 in group B . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00621))_
- In this case, the order of action announcements is A → B or B → A . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00621))_
- announcements are made by each group. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00621))_
- Which of the two will make their action announcements first is determined based on intelligence . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00622))_
- The group with the highest intelligence among all the characters (including monsters, etc.) participating in combat will announce their action later . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00622))_
- If a2 is the one with the highest intelligence between groups A and B as mentioned, then the announcements are made in the order of B → A , regardless of the intelligence of the other characters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00622))_
- Those who announce their actions later- the ones with highest representative intelligence --will have a significant advantage because they can announce their own actions after hearing their opponents' announcements. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00622))_
- If a2 is the one with the highest intelligence between groups A and B as mentioned, then the announcements are made in the order of B → A , regardless of the intelligence of the other characters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00622))_
- Those who announce their actions later- the ones with highest representative intelligence --will have a significant advantage because they can announce their own actions after hearing their opponents' announcements. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00622))_
- If the two groups are equal in intelligence (i.e., the highest intelligence in each group is the same), compare the second highest in each group. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00623))_
- If those are also the same, then compare the third highest. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00623))_
- If those are also the same, then compare the third highest. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00623))_
- The groups with the higher intelligence can decide on their actions later than the others. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00626))_
- If there are three or more groups instead of two, the order of action announcements is determined in the same way. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00626))_
- These impossible actions cannot be declared, as a matter of course. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00627))_
- If there's an enemy in front of you that's attacking you, attacking another enemy several meters away is not possible, just by common sense. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00627))_
- Casting a spell while running at full speed is prohibited by the rules. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00627))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00621))_

> announcements are made by each group. Suppose there are three characters, a1, a2, and a3 in group A and two characters, b1 and b2 in group B . In this case, the order of action announcements is A → B or B → A . Characters who belong to different groups, such as a1→b1→a2 ...... do not take turns to announce their actions.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00624))_

> If a settlement cannot be reached, the game master gets to announce their group's actions first (NPCs and monsters) and then the players follow.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00625))_

> The order in which action announcements are made within the same group (e.g., a1, a2, a3 within group A) may be determined freely and can also be changed from round to round.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00627))_

> In an action announcement, you cannot declare something impossible.

## 3.1.2 Player Action Announcements

- Instead of attack with my weapon , they should say attack the rightmost goblin with my sword . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00630))_
- Instead of use magic , they should say cast a fi reball spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00631))_
- If the game master asks a question, be sure to answer it clearly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00632))_

## 3.1.3 NPC and Monster Action Announcements

- Specific action announcements may not be made at this time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00634))_
- So, if a character cannot predict something, the player cannot know it either. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00636))_
- When you see it for the first time, you won't know how a monster you don't recognize will act, and when an enemy tries to cast a spell you don't recognize, you won't know what that spell does. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00636))_
- However, the players should only act from their characters' point of view. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00636))_
- This is the difference in position between the players and the game master. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00636))_
- The game master, as the person who manages the game, must know the actions of all the characters and monsters that appear. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00636))_
- However, the players should only act from their characters' point of view. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00636))_

## 3.2

## Action Order

- Combat will then actually take place. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00641))_
- Once action announcements are complete, it is time to resolve both friends' and foes' actions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00641))_
- Combat will then actually take place. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00641))_
- Therefore, the goblin will act before Ducard II. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00642))_
- Therefore, the goblin will act before Ducard II. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00642))_
- This is done in order of agility , friend and foe alike. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00643))_
- Therefore, it is possible to have a mixture of friends and foes, as in a1 → b1 → a2 ....... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00643))_
- Therefore, it is possible to have a mixture of friends and foes, as in a1 → b1 → a2 ....... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00643))_
- If, as a result of the action of someone with higher agility, someone with lower agility is unable to perform their intended action , it means that their action is no longer possible . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00644))_
- If, as a result of the action of someone with higher agility, someone with lower agility is unable to perform their intended action , it means that their action is no longer possible . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00644))_
- If two have exactly the same agility, their actions are considered to be simultaneous . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00645))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00651))_

> Suppose that Ducard II has an agility of 13, which is equal to that of a goblin (let's call him IIDash from now on, since he is a bit faster). In this case, both attacks are considered to be carried out simultaneously. During the course of the game, each attack check is made in the order of goblin → IIDash (or vice versa). Otherwise, it would be difficult to progress the game.

## Action Canceling

- A character may cancel and not perform their announced action. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00655))_
- If you fall down yourself early in the order, you will not be able to act. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00655))_
- For example, if an enemy that you intended to attack falls down due to the attack of a fellow player, no further attack is necessary. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00655))_
- For example, if an enemy that you intended to attack falls down due to the attack of a fellow player, no further attack is necessary. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00655))_
- Or, in the middle of a round, your companion may ask you to help out by giving money or goods. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00656))_
- In such a case, a character may cancel their intended action. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00657))_
- You also cannot switch from magic to a weapon attack because you intended to cast a spell but were blocked from chanting halfway through. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00658))_
- However, characters cannot switch their actions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00658))_
- You also cannot switch from magic to a weapon attack because you intended to cast a spell but were blocked from chanting halfway through. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00658))_
- The choice is basically this: either continue with the announced action or cancel it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00659))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00655))_

> A character may cancel and not perform their announced action. For example, if an enemy that you intended to attack falls down due to the attack of a fellow player, no further attack is necessary. If you fall down yourself early in the order, you will not be able to act.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00658))_

> Just because a companion has knocked down an enemy in front of you does not mean that you can attack a different enemy.

## Change Attack Targets

## 3.2.1 Delaying an Action

- Characters (including NPCs and monsters) do not have to act based on their agility and can instead delay their actions. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00666))_
- In other words, they will act only at the very end of the round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00668))_
- In such a case, a character may lower their action order to 0 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00668))_
- In other words, they will act only at the very end of the round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00668))_
- In this case, an intended attack on an enemy that is about to withdraw can be changed to another target that you're engaged with. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00670))_
- At this time, an attacker can choose any new target to switch to (from among those they're engaged with). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00671))_
- As we'll see later, if all the enemies you're engaged with attempt to withdraw (flee), even if they have high agility, you can still _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00672))_
- attack, and you will have a higher chance of hitting than usual. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00674))_
- Characters have three options for movement: full movement , normal movement , or stand still . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00676))_
- Each has different distances that can be traveled and different restrictions on actions that can be taken. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00676))_
- Final decisions are to be made by the game master. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00677))_
- For any other actions, please use common sense based on the time frame of 1 round = 10 seconds. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00677))_
- Below are some guidelines on how movement works and which actions can be taken. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00677))_

## Withdraw (Flee) From Engagement

- You can escape from an opponent within melee attack range. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00698))_

- In other words, there is always a chance for the enemy to attack a character before they withdraw. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00700))_

- In other words, there is always a chance for the enemy to attack a character before they withdraw. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00700))_

## Weapon and Armor Restrictions

- A very frail man, all skin and bones, dressed in heavy plate armor and wielding a long greatsword... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00741))_
- All joking aside, however, if we want to be realistic, weapons and armor used should be limited by a character's strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00742))_
- All weapons and armor have a required strength to use them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00742))_
- All joking aside, however, if we want to be realistic, weapons and armor used should be limited by a character's strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00742))_
- In other words, a character with 14 strength can wield a weapon with a required strength of 14, wield a shield with a required strength of 14, and wear armor with a required strength of 14. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00744))_
- A character's arms are largely divided into weapons, shields, and armor. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00744))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00742))_

> Characters may not use weapons or armor with a required strength greater than their own strength.

## Weapon Combat Skills

- To be good at weapon combat, a character must have skills. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00748))_

## 【 Fighter Skill 】

- The fighter skill is the most basic skill for weapon combat. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00751))_

## 【 Thief Skill 】

- Metal armor other than ring mail may not be used, even if the required strength (half of strength) is met. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00754))_
- A thief also has the ability to fight, but only with light equipment. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00754))_
- A thief also has the ability to fight, but only with light equipment. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00754))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00754))_

> Specifically, you can only fight if the weapon or armor you're using requires up to half (rounded up) of your character's strength.

## Projectile Restrictions

- There are some restrictions when handling projectiles such as bows and javelins. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00757))_
- There are some restrictions when handling projectiles such as bows and javelins. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00757))_
- There is no bow that has infinite reach. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00758))_
- 21) --is 100m , and of throwing weapons such as spearsthose marked throwable or made for throwing on the Weapons table --is 20m . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00758))_
- 21) --is 100m , and of throwing weapons such as spearsthose marked throwable or made for throwing on the Weapons table --is 20m . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00758))_
- If an enemy is beyond this distance, the projectile will not hit them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00759))_
- In addition, projectiles cannot be used in melee. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00760))_
- Weapons marked as throwable can also be used as melee weapons, but not as projectiles. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00760))_
- Again, movement is also restricted when using projectiles. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00761))_
- Of course, one-handed weapon + armor, two-handed weapon without armor, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00761))_
- However, you may not hold two weapons in both hands or hold a shield in each hand-- it is fine to do so, but a shield in your dominant hand or a weapon in your non-dominant hand is of no use. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00761))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00758))_

> One is their range . There is no bow that has infinite reach. The maximum of shooting weapons such as bows and arrows- those marked shooting on the Weapons table (see p. 21) --is 100m , and of throwing weapons such as spearsthose marked throwable or made for throwing on the Weapons table --is 20m .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00760))_

> When there's an enemy in front of you, you can never use a projectile marked shooting or made for throwing .

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00760))_

> In addition, projectiles cannot be used in melee. When there's an enemy in front of you, you can never use a projectile marked shooting or made for throwing . Weapons marked as throwable can also be used as melee weapons, but not as projectiles.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00761))_

> A character must choose stand still as their movement weapon + shield + armor or two-handed weapon + armor.

## 【 Ranger Skill 】

- There is no ability to attack with melee weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00763))_
- 21) - can they fight as well as a fighter when attacking. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00763))_
- The ranger skill cannot be used for defense. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00764))_

- method, and can only move up to 3 meters in 1 round . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00765))_
