> Weapons that can be used for both melee and throwing, as indicated by throwable on the Weapons table, are useful only when used for throwing, not for melee.

## Scores Used in Weapon Combat

- Attack power , strike power , bonus damage , critical target , evasion speed , defense power , and damage reduction . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00770))_

## 【 Attack Power 】

- This is the number needed to determine whether a character's attack will hit or not. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00772))_
- The higher this score, the higher the probability that a character's attack will hit. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00772))_
- If fighter (or thief, ranger) skill level is 0 , then attack power is 0 regardless of the character's dexterity bonus. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00775))_
- If fighter (or thief, ranger) skill level is 0 , then attack power is 0 regardless of the character's dexterity bonus. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00775))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00773))_

> Attack power is calculated with the following formula:

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00774))_

> attack power = fighter (thief/ranger) skill level + dexterity bonus

## 【 Strike Power 】

- This is the number needed to determine how much damage a character's attack will deal if it hits. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00777))_
- The greater this score, the more damage a character will deal. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00777))_
- However, if the weapon is a two-handed weapon, it's instead required strength + 5 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00778))_
- Strike power is the same number as the required strength of the weapon used. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00778))_
- If a character does not have a weapon (they're barehanded ), Strike Power is 0 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00780))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00777))_

> This is the number needed to determine how much damage a character's attack will deal if it hits. The greater this score, the more damage a character will deal.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00779))_

> strike power = required strength of weapon (+5 if a two-handed weapon)

## 【 Bonus Damage 】

- Characters with deep experience in combat can handle weapons effectively. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00782))_
- In addition, characters with high strength can wield weapons more powerfully. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00782))_
- This is represented by bonus damage . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00782))_
- If fighter (or thief, ranger) skill level is 0 , bonus damage is 0 regardless of the character's strength bonus. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00785))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00782))_

> These characters can deal more damage to their foes, even when using the same sized weapon.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00783))_

> Bonus damage is calculated with the following formula:

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00784))_

> bonus damage = fighter (thief/ranger) skill level + strength bonus

## 【 Critical Target 】

- The critical target is the number needed to determine whether a critical occurs. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00788))_
- In Sword World RPG , this is resolved with a process called a critical . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00788))_
- However, when attacking with the thief skill, it is instead 9 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00789))_
- Usually the critical target is 10 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00789))_
- The critical target is a lower is better score, which is rare in Sword World RPG . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00790))_
- Therefore, an attack using the thief skill is more likely to result in a critical than an attack using the regular fighter skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00790))_
- Therefore, an attack using the thief skill is more likely to result in a critical than an attack using the regular fighter skill. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00790))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00787))_

> If a weapon hits, it'll sometimes deal more damage than normal.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00790))_

> The critical target is a lower is better score, which is rare in Sword World RPG . Therefore, an attack using the thief skill is more likely to result in a critical than an attack using the regular fighter skill.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00791))_

> critical target = 10 (9 if using thief skill)

## 【 Evasion Speed 】

- In combat, characters are not the only ones who can attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00793))_
- To evade an enemy's attack, a score called evasion speed is necessary. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00793))_
- Naturally, the enemy will also attack the characters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00793))_
- In combat, characters are not the only ones who can attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00793))_
- A character with excellent evasion speed will be able to evade enemy attacks more easily. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00794))_
- evasion speed = fighter (thief) skill level + agility bonus If fighter (or thief) skill level is 0 , then evasion speed is 0 regardless of the character's agility bonus. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00795))_
- evasion speed = fighter (thief) skill level + agility bonus If fighter (or thief) skill level is 0 , then evasion speed is 0 regardless of the character's agility bonus. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00795))_
- A character holding a shield receives a +1 bonus to evasion speed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00796))_
- A fighter (or thief) skill level of 0 does not prevent you from receiving this shield bonus. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00797))_
- Therefore, the evasion speed of such a character will be 1 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00797))_
- Therefore, the evasion speed of such a character will be 1 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00797))_

## 【 Defense Power 】

- It is defense power that represents this. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00799))_
- Defense power is the same number as the required strength of the armor worn by the character. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00800))_
- If a character is not wearing any armor (just clothes or nothing at all), then defense power is 0 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00802))_
- If a character is not wearing any armor (just clothes or nothing at all), then defense power is 0 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00802))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00799))_

> If by a stroke of bad luck, an opponent's attack hits, a character can still reduce the damage by means of armor.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00801))_

> defense power = required strength of armor

## 【 Damage Reduction 】

- Those who are called heroes do not die from just a few injuries. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00804))_
- In Sword World RPG , a character with a high adventurer level, that is, a character who has a high level in any adventurer skill, is considered a hero . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00804))_
- Damage reduction is a score that reduces the damage a character would have taken. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00805))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00804))_

> Those who are called heroes do not die from just a few injuries. In Sword World RPG , a character with a high adventurer level, that is, a character who has a high level in any adventurer skill, is considered a hero . Therefore, we determine a hero's resistance to death by their adventurer level .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00806))_

> damage reduction = adventurer level

## Filling out the Character Sheet

- Please write these scores in the designated fields on the Character Sheet. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00808))_
- This will make the game run more smoothly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00808))_
- It is sufficient to write down each weapon, shield, and armor along with their required strength, as well as attack power, strike power, critical target, bonus damage, evasion speed, defense power, and damage reduction in the total field at the bottom. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00809))_
- Combat consists of two checks: one is made to determine if an attack hits , and if the total result is a hit, another is made to determine the damage dealt by the attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00810))_
- There is one other case, monster against monster , but since only the game master should know how to handle this case, we won't explain it here. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00811))_
- These procedures differ when the attacker and defender are character against monster , monster against character , or character against character . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00811))_
- There is one other case, monster against monster , but since only the game master should know how to handle this case, we won't explain it here. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00811))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00810))_

> Have you learned each of these terms? Now, let's get into the actual description of the combat procedure. Combat consists of two checks: one is made to determine if an attack hits , and if the total result is a hit, another is made to determine the damage dealt by the attack.

## Attacks From Characters Against Monsters

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00815))_

## 4.5.1 Hit Checks

- In other words, you roll 2D and add the result to your attack power (fighter/thief/ranger skill level + dexterity bonus) in order to meet or exceed a certain target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00818))_
- In the monsters' data, there is a score called evasion points , which becomes the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00819))_
- character's attack power + 2D < monster's evasion points → attack fails ※ Double sixes will automatically hit, and double ones will automatically fail. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00821))_
- Since a hit check is also a type of success roll, double ones is an automatic failure and double sixes is an automatic success . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00822))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00818))_

> In other words, you roll 2D and add the result to your attack power (fighter/thief/ranger skill level + dexterity bonus) in order to meet or exceed a certain target score.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00817))_

> When a character attacks a monster, they must make a success roll hit check, using attack power as the baseline score.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00818))_

> In other words, you roll 2D and add the result to your attack power (fighter/thief/ranger skill level + dexterity bonus) in order to meet or exceed a certain target score.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00820))_

> character's attack power + 2D ≥ monster's evasion points → attack hits

## Example:

- His attack power is 4 (fighter skill level 2, dexterity bonus 2) and the goblin's evasion points are 10. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00824))_
- Ducard II is fighting a goblin. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00824))_
- His player rolls the dice to make a hit check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00825))_
- 4+8= 12 , which is more than 10. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00825))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00825))_

> His player rolls the dice to make a hit check. The roll is 8. 4+8= 12 , which is more than 10. The attack hit the target perfectly.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00826))_

> Table 4-1: Rating Table

## 4.5.2

## Determining Base Damage (Strike Roll)

- Damage calculation takes three steps: ① Determine base damage , ② Add bonus damage , and ③ Subtract the monster's defense points . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00830))_
- The numbers are listed in a row, but of course there is no need to memorize them at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00832))_
- For example, if your character's strike power is 14, you only need to look at key number column 14. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00833))_
- For example, if your character's strike power is 14, you only need to look at key number column 14. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00833))_
- This process is called a strike roll . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00836))_
- This process is called a strike roll . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00836))_
- His weapon is a broadsword (a one-handed weapon) with required strength 14, so his strike power is 14 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00839))_
- Base damage is 4 points . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00840))_
- The result of his 2D roll is 6, so the number on row 6 under key number column 14 on the Rating Table is 4 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00840))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00829))_

> If your hit check is a success and you're able to hit your target, the damage dealt to your opponent can be determined next.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00830))_

> Damage calculation takes three steps: ① Determine base damage , ② Add bonus damage , and ③ Subtract the monster's defense points .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00831))_

> The first step is to determine base damage. Use Table 4-1: Rating Table to determine base damage.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00833, source-range-de1806a5-00836))_

> On the table, look at the column where the key number equals your character's strike power . For example, if your character's strike power is 14, you only need to look at key number column 14. The player should then roll 2D. Follow along the row next to the result and look for the number under the key number column that matches your character's strike power . That's the base damage . This process is called a strike roll .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00834))_

The player should then roll 2D. Follow along the row next to the result and look for the number under the key number column that matches your character's strike power . That's the base damage . This process is called a strike roll .

## 【 0 Damage! 】

- This means your attack hit the target, but failed to deal any damage! _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00843))_
- This means your attack hit the target, but failed to deal any damage! _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00843))_
- Maybe it hit the hardest part of their armor, or maybe it only hit the hilt of a spear or ax, but no damage was dealt. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00844))_
- Maybe it hit the hardest part of their armor, or maybe it only hit the hilt of a spear or ax, but no damage was dealt. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00844))_

## 【 Criticals

- In other words, you have landed a greater strike than would normally be expected. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00847))_
- Then, roll 2D again and look under the same key number on the Rating Table. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00848))_
- This is your base damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00848))_
- Then, roll 2D again and look under the same key number on the Rating Table. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00848))_
- This process can continue as long as you keep rolling greater than or equal to the critical target. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00849))_
- In theory, this could deal an infinite amount of damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00849))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00848))_

> Make a note of the score you got on the Rating Table. Then, roll 2D again and look under the same key number on the Rating Table. Add that result to the previous result. This is your base damage.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00847))_

> When making a strike roll, if your 2D roll is equal to or greater than the critical target (usually 10 , 9 if using the thief skill), the strike is critical .

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00848, source-range-de1806a5-00850))_

> Make a note of the score you got on the Rating Table. Then, roll 2D again and look under the same key number on the Rating Table. Add that result to the previous result. This is your base damage. If a critical occurs, but you roll double ones on the second and subsequent damage checks, 0 is added to the previous result (i.e., the previous result remains the same ), and the base damage check will end. It can never be 0 damage. Rest assured.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00849))_

> If the second 2D roll is also greater than or equal to the critical target, you can make a third roll to add even more damage.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00849))_

> If the second 2D roll is also greater than or equal to the critical target, you can make a third roll to add even more damage. This process can continue as long as you keep rolling greater than or equal to the critical target. In theory, this could deal an infinite amount of damage.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00850))_

> If a critical occurs, but you roll double ones on the second and subsequent damage checks, 0 is added to the previous result (i.e., the previous result remains the same ), and the base damage check will end.

## Example:

- Ducard II's critical target is 10. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00852))_
- Suppose that Ducard Ⅱ , having hit the goblin with an attack, rolls a 10 when determining base damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00852))_
- The number on row 10 under key number column 14 on the Rating Table is 6 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00852))_
- We'll make a note of the 6 and roll the dice a second time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00852))_
- Since the number on row 6 is 4 , the base damage dealt by Ducard Ⅱ is 6+8+4= 18 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00853))_
- This did not reach the critical target, so the base damage check ends here. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00853))_
- The number on row 12 is 8 , so we'll make a note of the 8 and roll a third time. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00853))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00853))_

> That's still greater than the critical target. The number on row 12 is 8 , so we'll make a note of the 8 and roll a third time. The roll is 6 . This did not reach the critical target, so the base damage check ends here. Since the number on row 6 is 4 , the base damage dealt by Ducard Ⅱ is 6+8+4= 18 .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00854))_

> If the third roll were a 2 (double ones), the base damage would instead be 6+8+0= 14 .

## 4.5.3 Bonus Damage

- This will be the total amount of damage the character deals the monster. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00856))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00856))_

> Once you've determined base damage, you must then add your character's bonus damage.

## Example:

- Ducard II's bonus damage is 4 (fighter skill level 2, strength bonus 2). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00858))_
- No matter how much bonus damage you have, it means nothing. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00862))_
- No matter how much bonus damage you have, it means nothing. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00862))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00859))_

> If base damage is 4, the total damage is 4+4= 8 ,

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00860))_

> If base damage is 18, the total damage is 18+4= 22 ,

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00861))_

> If base damage is 14, the total damage is 14+4= 18 .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00862))_

> When determining base damage (strike roll), if you suddenly roll double ones, the damage will always be 0 .

## 4.5.4

## Monster Defense Points

- Monsters can repel and hold off attacks with their thick skins and hard scales. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00865))_
- This indicates the amount of damage a monster can reduce. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00865))_
- Please note that a monster's defense points are treated differently from a character's defense power . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00865))_
- In monster data, this is represented by defense points . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00865))_

## Example:

- The goblin can prevent 4 points of damage with its skin. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00867))_
- A goblin has 4 defense points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00867))_

## 4.5.5 Final Damage

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00870))_

> final damage = base damage + bonus damage - monster's defense points

## Attacks From Monsters Against Characters

## 4.6.1 Hit (Evasion) Checks

- This check is also made with a success roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00877))_
- The baseline score is the character's evasion speed . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00878))_
- The target score is still different for each monster, but it's called the monster's attack points . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00878))_
- ※ Double sixes will automatically evade, and double ones will automatically fail _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00881))_
- Since this is a success roll, double sixes and double ones are automatic successes and automatic failures , respectively. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00882))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00877))_

> This check is also made with a success roll.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00879))_

> evasion speed + 2D ≥ monster's attack points → evasion success (monster's attack fails)

## Example:

- Ducard II has an evasion speed of 5 (fighter level 2, agility bonus +2, has a shield). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00884))_
- On the other hand, the goblin has 10 attack points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00884))_
- Ducard II is hit by the goblin. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00885))_
- The player rolls the dice to determine if they evade the goblin's attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00885))_
- 5+4= 9 , which is short of the target score (the goblin's attack points) of 10. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00885))_

## 4.6.2 Damage Dealt by

## Monsters

- Strike points are fixed and do not change. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00889))_
- This is determined for each monster by the monster's strike points . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00889))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00888))_

> If a monster's attack hits (or if a character fails to evade), you must look for the base damage dealt by the monster.

## Example:

- A goblin has 7 strike points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00891))_

## 4.6.3 Defense Rolls

- For example, if your defense power is 7, you would look at key number column 7 with on the Rating Table . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00893))_
- You can determine the amount of damage reduction by using Table 4-1: Rating Table. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00893))_
- For example, if your defense power is 7, you would look at key number column 7 with on the Rating Table . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00893))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00893))_

> Characters can reduce the damage they receive from monsters by means of armor.

## Table 4-3: Rating Table, Key Number Column 7

- This process is specifically called a defense roll . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00896))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00896))_

> Roll 2D and follow the row next to the result. Then, look for the number under the appropriate column. This process is specifically called a defense roll .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00895))_

> [Figure] (p.47)

## Example:

- Since Ducard II has a defense power of 7 , we'll use key number column 7. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00898))_
- The number on row 3 under key number column 7 is 0 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00898))_
- This is the damage that Ducard II is able to prevent with his armor. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00898))_

## 【 Failure! 】

- In this case, your character cannot reduce the monster's damage (strike points) at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00900))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00900))_

> When making a defense roll, if your roll is double ones, that means that you've been hit extremely hard.

## 【 Criticals? 】

- No matter how high the roll, only the amount of damage reduction indicated by that roll is used, nothing is added to it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00902))_
- No matter how high the roll, only the amount of damage reduction indicated by that roll is used, nothing is added to it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00902))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00902))_

> When making a defense roll, criticals do not occur like they do with a strike roll.

## Example:

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00904))_

> You cannot roll the dice again to increase this number further.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00904))_

> Suppose Ducard II's defense roll came up 12 (double sixes!). The number on row 12 under key number column 7 is 6 . Unlike a strike roll, a defense roll is made only once. You cannot roll the dice again to increase this number further.

## 4.6.4 Damage Reduction

- The result of your defense roll plus this damage reduction equals the fi nal amount of damage your character can reduce. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00906))_
- Characters can also reduce damage using their adventurer level . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00906))_
- No matter how high your character's damage reduction (adventurer level), that damage cannot be reduced at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00907))_
- However, if the result of your defense roll is double ones , your character's damage reduction is meaningless . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00907))_

## 4.6.5 Final Damage

- The final damage your character suffers from a monster equals the monster's strike points minus the result of your defense roll plus your character's damage reduction . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00909))_
- If the defense roll came up double ones , no such calculation would be made, and the goblin's 7 strike points would be the damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00912))_
- If final damage is 0 or negative , that means you took no damage at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00913))_
- The monster's attack was completely blocked by your armor. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00913))_
- If final damage is 0 or negative , that means you took no damage at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00913))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00909))_

> The final damage your character suffers from a monster equals the monster's strike points minus the result of your defense roll plus your character's damage reduction .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00910))_

> Final damage = monster's strike points - (defense roll result + damage reduction)

## 4.7

## Attacks From Characters Against Characters

- While infighting between player characters is something you should avoid at all costs, fights against nonplayer characters are common. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00918))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00918))_

> Sometimes, especially when the mastermind behind an incident is a dark priest, you'll have to fight against someone whose data was created for a character.

## 4.7.1 Hit Checks

- The attacker adds their roll to their attack power , and the defender adds their roll to their evasion speed . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00921))_
- If there is a tie or the defender's is greater, the attack fails . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00924))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00921))_

> To make a hit check, both sides must roll the dice (2D).

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00921))_

> To make a hit check, both sides must roll the dice (2D). The attacker adds their roll to their attack power , and the defender adds their roll to their evasion speed .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00922))_

> attacker's final score = attack power + 2D

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00921))_

> To make a hit check, both sides must roll the dice (2D). The attacker adds their roll to their attack power , and the defender adds their roll to their evasion speed .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00923))_

> defender's final score = evasion speed + 2D

## hits

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00927))_

> attacker's attack power + 2D ≤ defender's evasion speed +2D → attack fails

## Example:

- Ducard II 's attack power is 4 , while Bucky's evasion speed is 5 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00929))_
- Ducard II has higher agility, so he attacks first. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00929))_
- Ducard II rolls a 7 , and Bucky rolls a 6 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00930))_
- In combat between characters, a tie is considered an attack failure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00932))_
- Ducard II 's attack was narrowly dodged. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00932))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00931))_

> Ducard II's final score is 4+7= 11 , while Bucky's final score is 5+6= 11 . It's a tie.

## Double Sixes and Double Ones

- If a roll comes up double sixes or double ones during combat between characters, the situation is resolved as follows: _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00934))_
- If the attacker's roll is double ones , the attack fails without question. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00935))_
- However, if the defender's roll is also double sixes, the attack fails . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00936))_
- If the attacker's roll is double sixes , it' s basically an automatic hit. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00936))_
- If the defender's roll is double ones , it' s basically an automatic hit. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00937))_
- However, if the attacker's roll is also double ones, the attack fails . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00937))_
- It doesn't matter even if the attacker rolls double sixes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00938))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00934))_

> If a roll comes up double sixes or double ones during combat between characters, the situation is resolved as follows:

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00935))_

> The defender doesn't even have to roll the dice.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00937))_

> If the defender's roll is double ones , it' s basically an automatic hit. However, if the attacker's roll is also double ones, the attack fails .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00938))_

> If the defender's roll is double sixes , the attack will always fail.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00938))_

> If the defender's roll is double sixes , the attack will always fail. It doesn't matter even if the attacker rolls double sixes.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00939))_

> Please refer to Table 4-4: Attack Checks.

## 4.7.2 Damage Checks

- This procedure is exactly the same for characters as it was during combat with monsters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00941))_
- The difference is the damage dealt to the defender. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00944))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00943))_

> The defender must make a defense roll, then add their damage reduction to the result to determine how much damage they reduced.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00941))_

> If your attack hits, you must then determine damage.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00941, source-range-de1806a5-00943))_

> If your attack hits, you must then determine damage. This procedure is exactly the same for characters as it was during combat with monsters. The defender must make a defense roll, then add their damage reduction to the result to determine how much damage they reduced.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00942))_

> The attacker must make a strike roll to determine their base damage, then add their bonus damage.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00942))_

> The attacker must make a strike roll to determine their base damage, then add their bonus damage.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00943))_

> The defender must make a defense roll, then add their damage reduction to the result to determine how much damage they reduced.

## Example:

- The result of his strike roll is 11 (base damage 9 ). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00947))_
- His following 2D roll is 9 (base damage 7 ). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00947))_
- His bonus damage is 5 (fighter skill level 3, strength bonus +2), which brings the total amount of damage to 9+7+5= 21 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00947))_
- Bucky is wielding a battle-ax (required strength 15) with both hands, so he has a strike power of 20 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00947))_
- After adding his damage reduction of 2 , 3+2= 5 points is the final amount of damage Ducard II is able to reduce. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00948))_
- His defense roll result is 7 , and his armor's defense power is 7, so the damage that'll be reduced by his armor is 3 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00948))_
- After adding his damage reduction of 2 , 3+2= 5 points is the final amount of damage Ducard II is able to reduce. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00948))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00948))_

> Ducard II now determines how much damage his armor prevents. His defense roll result is 7 , and his armor's defense power is 7, so the damage that'll be reduced by his armor is 3 . After adding his damage reduction of 2 , 3+2= 5 points is the final amount of damage Ducard II is able to reduce.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00949))_

> As a result, the damage that Ducard II suffers is 21-5= 16 points .

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00948))_

> Ducard II now determines how much damage his armor prevents. His defense roll result is 7 , and his armor's defense power is 7, so the damage that'll be reduced by his armor is 3 . After adding his damage reduction of 2 , 3+2= 5 points is the final amount of damage Ducard II is able to reduce.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00951))_

> [Figure] (p.49)

## 4.8

## Applying Damage

- This reduction is temporary and can be recovered naturally or by magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00955))_
- If you suffer damage and your life force (points) is reduced, then suffer more damage, your life force (points) will be further reduced from there. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00956))_
- If you suffer damage and your life force (points) is reduced, then suffer more damage, your life force (points) will be further reduced from there. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00956))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00957))_

> After damage is applied, a character (monster) can still act if they have at least 1 life force (point) remaining.

## 4.9

## Unconscious and Death Checks

- Also, that character (monster) is now in danger of dying . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00961))_
- It is no longer possible for them to act. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00961))_
- If, as a result of damage (accumulated damage), a character's (monster's) life force (points) becomes 0 or negative , that character (monster) will fall unconscious . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00961))_

## Example:

- As a result, Ducard II now has a life force of -4 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00963))_
- Ducard II, with 2 points of life force left, was hit by Bucky's ax again in the next round, suffering 6 points of damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00963))_

## 4.9.1

## Adventurer Death Checks

- Any adventurer who falls to 0 or negative life force is now at risk of dying . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00966))_
- This check is made using a life force resistance roll . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00967))_
- The baseline score is life force resistance (adventurer level + life force bonus) and the target score is 7 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00967))_
- If the character's life force is negative , the negative amount will be used as a penalty . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00967))_
- life force resistance + 2D -(damage applied beyond life force) < 7 → death _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00969))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00966))_

> You must roll the dice to determine if your character survives.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00967))_

> This check is made using a life force resistance roll . The baseline score is life force resistance (adventurer level + life force bonus) and the target score is 7 . If the character's life force is negative , the negative amount will be used as a penalty .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00968))_

> life force resistance + 2D - (damage applied beyond life force) ≥ 7 → survival

## Example:

- Since Ducard II has a life force of -4 , that will also be the penalty. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00971))_
- His life force resistance is 5 (adventurer level 2, life force bonus +3). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00971))_
- His result after rolling 2D is 7 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00971))_
- His result after rolling 2D is 7 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00971))_
- If the result of this death check had been 6 or lower, Ducard II would have met an untimely end . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00972))_
- 5 (life force resistance) + 7 (roll) - 4 (penalty) = 8 , which is greater or equal than 7. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00972))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00972))_

> 5 (life force resistance) + 7 (roll) - 4 (penalty) = 8 , which is greater or equal than 7. Ducard II survives. If the result of this death check had been 6 or lower, Ducard II would have met an untimely end .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00971))_

> Ducard II, with a life force of -4 , must make a death check .

## 4.9.2 Death Checks Every Hour

- Characters with 0 or negative life force remain unconscious, even if they successfully survived a death check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00974))_
- and have their life force increased by up to at least 1 (positive) point, they will regain consciousness, but if they're left unconscious, they may die. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00974))_
- The target score is still 7 , and if their life force is still negative , it' s still a penalty . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00975))_
- If the result is double sixes (automatic success), the character will regain consciousness on their own. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00976))_
- At this time, their life force becomes 1 point. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00976))_
- If the result is any success other than double sixes, the character is alive _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00977))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00976))_

> If the result is double sixes (automatic success), the character will regain consciousness on their own. At this time, their life force becomes 1 point.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00975))_

> Characters with 0 or negative life force must make a death check every hour .

## Example:

- Ducard II is hit by Bucky's attack and suffers 16 points of damage, reducing his life force to 2 points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00979))_
- Ducard II has 18 life force. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00979))_
- If the result is failure, the character will die. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00981))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00981))_

> If the result is failure, the character will die.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00980))_

> In one hour , they must make another death check.

## 4.9.3 Monster Death Check

- A score called the monster's life point resistance is used instead. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00983))_
- Subtract the amount of negative life points, if any, from life point resistance, and if the result is 7 or greater , the monster is alive . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00983))_
- If the result is 6 or lower , the monster is dead . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00983))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00983))_

> Monsters with 0 or negative life points must also make a check to see if they live or die.

## Example:

- A goblin's life point resistance score is 10 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00985))_
- A goblin, Scarnose, was hit by an adventurer's sword and fell down with -6 life points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00985))_
- 10-6= 4 , which is less than 7 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00985))_
- If it's alive after a death check, it recovers 1 life point every hour , and will regain consciousness when it reaches (positive) 1 life point. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00986))_
- If it's alive after a death check, it recovers 1 life point every hour , and will regain consciousness when it reaches (positive) 1 life point. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00986))_

## 4.9.4 Mercy

- However, to do so, their weapon must be a dull weapon (a bludgeoning weapon). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00988))_
- On Table 1-8: Weapons, maces , clubs , and staves are classified as dull weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00988))_
- However, even if an item is classified as one of these, if you throw it, you cannot declare mercy. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00988))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00988))_

> If a character does not want to kill their opponent, they can declare mercy .

- mercy when using a barehanded attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00990))_

- It cannot be done after a hit or after damage check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00990))_

- A character who falls down with 0 or negative life force due to an attack that was declared to be mercy , will only fail their death check if double ones are rolled. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00991))_

## Excellent Weapons and Armor

- A variety of weapons and armor can be found in Forcelia. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00995))_
- Some are of high quality made by master craftsmen, some have magical powers, and others are made of unusual materials such as silver or mithril silver. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00995))_
- Some are of high quality made by master craftsmen, some have magical powers, and others are made of unusual materials such as silver or mithril silver. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00995))_

## 4.10.1 High-Quality Weapons

## and Armor

- There are people in the world who are called master craftsmen, and they sometimes create weapons and armor that are better than normal. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00999))_
- These highquality weapons and armor are available to adventurers, if they seek them out. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-00999))_
- As a result, this broadsword can be wielded by someone with 15 strength, but still has a strike power of 16. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01000))_
- For example, a broadsword that originally had a required strength 16 could be made with required strength 15 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01000))_
- High-quality weapons and armor are easier to handle, and are made with lower required strength than usual. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01000))_
- For example, a broadsword that originally had a required strength 16 could be made with required strength 15 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01000))_
- It is important to note that such weapons and armor cannot exceed the limits of the weapon or armor itself. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01001))_
- For example, if you want a high-quality broadsword, its required strength (size) is naturally limited to 8-16 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01001))_
- Since there is no such broadsword with a natural required strength of 17, that means there is no such thing as a broadsword with required strength 16 and strike power 17 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01001))_
- It is important to note that such weapons and armor cannot exceed the limits of the weapon or armor itself. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01001))_
- Since there is no such broadsword with a natural required strength of 17, that means there is no such thing as a broadsword with required strength 16 and strike power 17 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01001))_
- For example, if you want a high-quality broadsword, its required strength (size) is naturally limited to 8-16 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01001))_
- Y ou will not see any with a required strength that's 6 lower than usual . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01002))_
- So long as required strength is up to 5 lower than usual, a character can purchase one at a store. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01003))_
- For reference, a guideline is printed here (Table 4-5: Guideline for Obtaining High-Quality Weapons and Armor). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01003))_
- If successful, then even if it's not double sixes, they will regain consciousness with 1 point of life force. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01005))_
- If successful, then even if it's not double sixes, they will regain consciousness with 1 point of life force. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01005))_
- If left alone, it will always regain consciousness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01006))_
- In addition, no matter how high quality it is, nothing can affect its attack power or evasion speed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01007))_
- It'll also have no effect on monsters that can only be wounded by magic or magical weapons (such as spirits, beastmen, etc.). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01007))_
- It'll also have no effect on monsters that can only be wounded by magic or magical weapons (such as spirits, beastmen, etc.). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01007))_

For example, if you want a high-quality broadsword, its required strength (size) is naturally limited to 8-16 . Since there is no such broadsword with a natural required strength of 17, that means there is no such thing as a broadsword with required strength 16 and strike power 17 .

## 4.10.2

## Silver Weapons and Armor

- It is also one of only two types of metal that's known to exist in the spirit world . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01010))_
- Silver is a special metal, imbued with magical powers. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01010))_
- It is also one of only two types of metal that's known to exist in the spirit world . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01010))_
- Even silver weapons and armor have no differences in their strike power, defense power, attack power, or evasion speed at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01011))_
- However, silver weapons , due to their magic power, can deal damage to monsters that can only be wounded by magic or magical weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01012))_
- However, silver weapons , due to their magic power, can deal damage to monsters that can only be wounded by magic or magical weapons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01012))_
- Also, shamans are not normally able to wear metal armor (as metal armor prevents them from using spirit magic), but they can use spirit magic while wearing it, if it's silver armor . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01013))_
- Silver weapons and armor will also cost 5 times the original price. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01014))_
- For example, the price of a silver item with a required strength 2 times lower than the original would be 5 x 2 = 10 times the price of the original. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01014))_
- Y ou can also ask for a high-quality item of silver, but the price will be even higher . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01014))_
- To buy silver weapons and armor in a store, you must be in a town about the size of the royal city level in a small country. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01014))_
- For example, the price of a silver item with a required strength 2 times lower than the original would be 5 x 2 = 10 times the price of the original. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01014))_

## 4.10.3

## Magical Weapons and Armor

- These magical weapons and armor are seldom found, and are usually rarely traded. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01017))_
- In Forcelia, there are magical weapons and armor that were once created during the age of the magical kingdom (Kastuul). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01017))_
- Characters may obtain these magical weapons and armor through their adventures. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01018))_
- The power of magical weapons and armor is represented by a +1 or +2 score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01018))_
- In Forcelia, the limit for this power is +3 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01019))_
- There are only a scant few weapons and armor in the world with +3 power, and any with +4 or more have yet to be found. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01019))_
- There are only a scant few weapons and armor in the world with +3 power, and any with +4 or more have yet to be found. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01019))_
- In Forcelia, the limit for this power is +3 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01019))_

## 【 Magical Weapons 】

- If you use a +1 magical weapon, you receive a +1 bonus to attack power and bonus damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01021))_
- Magical weapons have exceptional attack power and bonus damage . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01021))_
- If +2, those bonuses are +2, and if +3, they're +3. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01021))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01021))_

> Magical weapons have exceptional attack power and bonus damage . If you use a +1 magical weapon, you receive a +1 bonus to attack power and bonus damage. If +2, those bonuses are +2, and if +3, they're +3.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01022))_

> Magical weapons can deal damage to monsters that normal weapons cannot harm.

## 【 Magical Shields 】

- Magical shields increase evasion speed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01024))_
- A +1 magic shield grants +1 evasion speed, +2 if +2. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01024))_

## 【 Magical Armor 】

- Magical armor increases evasion speed and damage reduction . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01026))_
- It does not reduce damage caused by magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01026))_
- It does not reduce damage caused by magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01026))_
- Also, they offer basically no bonus to strike power or defense power, same as required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01028))_
- Magical weapons and armor are not usually traded, but if you are very lucky you may be able to buy them if they're +1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01028))_
- High-quality ones (made with lower required strength) seem to exist only rarely . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01029))_
- High-quality ones (made with lower required strength) seem to exist only rarely . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01029))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01026))_

> If you wear +1 magical armor, you gain +1 to evasion speed and damage reduction.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01027))_

> Even if it is magical, if it is metal armor, a shaman cannot wear it.

## 4.10.4

## Mithril Silver Weapons and Armor

- Mithril silver , also known as true silver , is produced in very few places in the world. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01032))_
- However, the art of forging mithril silver was lost with the fall of the magical kingdom , so it has not been handed down to the present day. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01032))_
- If a character ever sees them, mithril silver weapons and armor are definitely a legacy from the era of the magical kingdom. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01033))_
- Mithril silver weapons and armor are always magical items and, like magical weapons and armor, have a power of +1 or +2. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01034))_
- This power affects attack power, evasion speed, bonus damage, and damage reduction. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01034))_
- No one has yet discovered anything with a power of +4 or higher. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01034))_
- Mithril silver is also a very strong metal. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01035))_
- For this reason, strike power and defense power are calculated by adding +5 to required strength. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01035))_
- Therefore, you can find a broadsword clad in mithril silver that has required strength 16 and strike power 21 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01036))_
- What makes them different from high-quality weapons and armor is that they do not lower required strength but rather increase strike power and defense power . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01036))_
- Therefore, you can find a broadsword clad in mithril silver that has required strength 16 and strike power 21 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01036))_
- Mithril silver and high-quality-- such as a broadsword made of mithril silver, originally with required strength 16 but made with 5 less , and also with +5 strike power because it' s mithril silver, resulting in required strength 11 and strike power 21 --is almost always rare. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01037))_
- Mithril silver and high-quality-- such as a broadsword made of mithril silver, originally with required strength 16 but made with 5 less , and also with +5 strike power because it' s mithril silver, resulting in required strength 11 and strike power 21 --is almost always rare. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01037))_
- Mithril silver, like regular silver, is a metal that exists in the spirit world. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01039))_
- Therefore, a shaman can wear mithril silver armor and still use spirit magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01039))_
- Therefore, a shaman can wear mithril silver armor and still use spirit magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01039))_
- Mithril silver chain mail does not interfere with the thief or ranger skills. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01040))_
- Chain mail made of mithril silver does not inhibit bodily movement as much as that made of steel (but splint armor, lamellar armor, and plate armor still do ). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01040))_
- It should be noted that, although this does not affect the numeric aspects of the game, mithril silver can be made to emit a variety of colors depending on how it's processed, and these colors will never fade. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01042))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01032))_

> Mithril silver is itself a mineral imbued with magic, and when processed, it gains power.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01037, source-range-de1806a5-01039))_

> Mithril silver and high-quality-- such as a broadsword made of mithril silver, originally with required strength 16 but made with 5 less , and also with +5 strike power because it' s mithril silver, resulting in required strength 11 and strike power 21 --is almost always rare. Mithril silver, like regular silver, is a metal that exists in the spirit world. Therefore, a shaman can wear mithril silver armor and still use spirit magic.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01038))_

> Mithril silver weapons can deal damage to monsters that normal weapons cannot harm.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01039))_

> Mithril silver, like regular silver, is a metal that exists in the spirit world. Therefore, a shaman can wear mithril silver armor and still use spirit magic.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01041))_

> Mithril silver weapons and armor are never , if ever, placed in shops or traded for money.

## Forger of Colors

- Dwarves in particular excelled in their craft, and powerful and beautiful mithril silver items made by dwarves can still be found in Alecrast even today. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01044))_
- The time of the ancient kingdom was a time of magical civilization far more advanced than today. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01044))_
- The following is an excerpt from a travelog entitled The Natural History of Alecrast , compiled by Laverna, a witch of Orphan, who toured the continent of Alecrast, and tells the episode of Somska, a dwarven craftsman said to be the best in the ancient kingdom. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01045))_
- The many mithril silver objects dating back to the ancient kingdom period are a marvel to us with their variety of colors and brilliancy. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01046))_
- Since I was told that showing off such a thing would only make me a target for thieves, I have rarely used this pen and have put it away, but when I occasionally take it out, I can see that it has not lost any of the beautiful contrast between red and yellow. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01046))_
- The "Crimson" breastplate is said to glow even redder than the blood of enemies, and the "White Sword" given to the commander of the kingsguard of Ramliearth does not lose its whiteness, no matter how much blood is spilled. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01046))_
- I myself was given a beautifully crafted pen in the shape of a feather by the king at the start of my journey. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01046))_
- Since I was told that showing off such a thing would only make me a target for thieves, I have rarely used this pen and have put it away, but when I occasionally take it out, I can see that it has not lost any of the beautiful contrast between red and yellow. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01046))_
- However, even today, on rare occasions, mithril silver ore is found, but it is black and has no sparkle whatsoever. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01047))_
- Mithril silver is said to acquire different colors and brilliance only when one draws out its power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01047))_
- Depending on the skill of the craftsman, it seems that different colors could be shown in a single item, as in my pen. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01047))_
- Mithril silver is said to acquire different colors and brilliance only when one draws out its power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01047))_
- The following anecdote is one of them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01049))_
- It's been said that no one could surpass the dwarves when it comes to the processing of mithril silver. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01049))_
- The quality and quantity of Somska's mithril work was said to be outstanding, and countless legends have been passed down. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01049))_
- Among the dwarves, the one who is said to have been the most skilled in the processing of mithril silver in the ancient kingdom, was Somska, an enchanter called the "Forger of Colors". _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01049))_
- One day, one of these great lords heard that the other had ordered a crown made, adorned with a hundred precious stones, and decided to have a crown made for himself as well. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01050))_
- The great lord ordered, "Make a crown with all your skill and magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01050))_
- It is said that there were two great lords, both very vain. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01050))_
- The two were strongly conscious of one another, and when one of them found a beautiful wife, the other sought out a beautiful girl, and when one threw a grand feast, the other would compete by throwing one twice as grand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01050))_
- Whoever makes the most beautiful crown will receive any reward they desire." _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01050))_
- The great lord gathered the famous enchanters and gave them mithril silver. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01050))_
- Somska silently accepted the lump of mithril silver and resigned his seat. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01051))_
- Somska was also summoned and given mithril silver. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01051))_
- Somska was also the one that the great lord relied on the most. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01051))_
- Finally, the last one, the crown of Somska, was presented. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01052))_
- The crowns were all wonderfully made. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01052))_
- The great lord sighed loudly and contentedly at each of the crowns. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01052))_
- The great lord expected a crown that would be greater than any he had ever received before. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01052))_
- A month passed, and the enchanters came before the great lord, each carrying a crown. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01052))_
- The great lord expected a crown that would be greater than any he had ever received before. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01052))_
- A month passed, and the enchanters came before the great lord, each carrying a crown. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01052))_
- The great lord was surprised, angry, and exclaimed, "Somska, what is this?! _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01053))_
- But what Somska offered was a black, gleamless crown with no trace of processing. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01053))_
- And, in fact, I also felt that the dwarves might have an exceptional sense of humor. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01054))_
- But like Somska, the dwarves will not reveal that smile behind their beards. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-01054))_
