> To resolve this, the one attempting to pin down must make a success roll using fi ghter/thief skill level + strength bonus as the baseline score, while the one resisting must make one using adventurer level + strength bonus .

## 4. Using Holy Magic or Common Runes

- It's usually a ring, so you can use it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08971))_
- Common runes require you to not only be able to speak, but to also be able to use a catalyst. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08971))_
- It's not possible while being strangled , but if you're merely being pinned down , you can use holy magic. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08971))_
- Common runes require you to not only be able to speak, but to also be able to use a catalyst. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08971))_
- Ancient magic, spirit magic, and spellsongs cannot be used. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08972))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08971))_

> Even while grappling, you can use holy magic so long as you can speak.

## 5. Escape from Grapple

- If the one trying to escape's final score is greater, or if there's a tie, the grapple will be broken. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08974))_
- Both sides will fall prone , with a short distance between them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08974))_
- To escape from a grapple, make a success roll check using adventurer level + agility bonus as the baseline score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08974))_
- Weapons you dropped when entering the grapple will probably be lying around nearby. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08974))_
- An opponent who is trying to maintain the grapple may counter this by making one using adventurer level + agility bonus or adventure level + strength bonus as the baseline score, whichever is greater. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08974))_
- If the strangler or pinner attempts to escape, he or she will automatically succeed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08975))_
- It' s fine for the game master to have certain monsters attempt to grapple. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08976))_
- For adventurers who rely on weapons, this may pose a threat. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08976))_
- Of course, a monster that does not have hands cannot strangle . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08976))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08974))_

> To escape from a grapple, make a success roll check using adventurer level + agility bonus as the baseline score. An opponent who is trying to maintain the grapple may counter this by making one using adventurer level + agility bonus or adventure level + strength bonus as the baseline score, whichever is greater. If the one trying to escape's final score is greater, or if there's a tie, the grapple will be broken. Both sides will fall prone , with a short distance between them. Weapons you dropp

> Those being strangled or pinned down cannot attempt to escape from a grapple unless they fi rst escape from those conditions.

## Attacks and Magic Against Grapplers

- Even if this attack misses, it will not hit your allies. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08978))_
- If you roll 1~3, you can aim at your enemy. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08978))_
- At this time, your enemy suffers a penalty (-2) for being prone . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08978))_
- This is because both your enemies and allies are fervently moving around. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08979))_
- However, if your enemy is being strangled or pinned down , you can attack them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08979))_
- This is because both your enemies and allies are fervently moving around. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08979))_
- Please make a damage check immediately. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08980))_
- If you roll a 6, you will unfortunately attack your ally by accident. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08980))_

> When attacking a target who is grappling from outside with a melee weapon, roll 1D. If you roll 1~3, you can aim at your enemy. Perform a hit check as normal. At this time, your enemy suffers a penalty (-2) for being prone . Even if this attack misses, it will not hit your allies.

> If you roll 4~5, you cannot attack.

## Ram

- This can only be performed with the fi ghter skill , it cannot be used with the thief skill . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08983))_
- If the attacker's final score is greater, the opponent will fall prone . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08983))_
- The attacker must make a success roll using fi ghter skill level + strength bonus as the baseline score, while the defender must make one using either adventurer level + strength bonus or adventurer level + life force bonus ( life point resistance -7 for monsters), whichever is greater. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08983))_
- This is an attack method where you knock an opponent prone by charging forward. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08983))_
- If your attack hits, make a strike power 0 and critical target 12 damage check. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08983))_
- This can only be performed with the fi ghter skill , it cannot be used with the thief skill . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08983))_
- If you use this optional rule, then if successfully hit by a monster whose attack method is ram , a character must determine whether he or she has fallen prone . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08985))_
- If you use this optional rule, then if successfully hit by a monster whose attack method is ram , a character must determine whether he or she has fallen prone . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08985))_

- This is an attack method that targets a specific body part of an opponent . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08987))_

- If it hits and further deals damage, there is a chance that your opponent will fall unconscious . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08990))_
- Monsters with mental attack immunity cannot be knocked unconscious. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08992))_

## Arm Aim

- Since you can only aim at one arm at a time, you cannot make your opponent drop a two-handed weapon using this method (if you make two attacks with two weapons, each aimed at a different arm, and hit, they'll drop both if they fail both resistance rolls, as described below). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08995))_
- If he or she fails, they'll drop what they were holding. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08995))_
- Your opponent must make a life force (points) resistance roll against a target score of 10 and with damage dealt to life force as a penalty. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08995))_
- Since you can only aim at one arm at a time, you cannot make your opponent drop a two-handed weapon using this method (if you make two attacks with two weapons, each aimed at a different arm, and hit, they'll drop both if they fail both resistance rolls, as described below). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08995))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08995))_

> If it hits and further deals damage, your opponent may drop what he or she was holding .

## Leg Aim

- Your opponent must make a life force (points) resistance roll against a target score of 10 and with damage dealt to life force (points) as a penalty. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08998))_
- If he or she fails, they will fall prone . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-08998))_

## Weapon Aim

- If it hits, your opponent must make a success roll against target score 10, using adventurer level + strength bonus as the baseline score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09001))_
- At this time, if the defender is holding a two-handed weapon, he or she will gain a +2 bonus. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09001))_
- On the other hand, this success roll also suffers a penalty depending on how forcefully the attacker's weapon strikes. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09002))_
- However, this strike roll cannot result in a critical . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09004))_
- - Make a strike roll the same as you would for a normal attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09004))_
- - Add the attacker's bonus damage to the result in 1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09005))_
- This final result is the penalty for the success roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09006))_
- Aiming for a weapon will be in vain against monsters that attack with fangs and claws on their bodies. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09007))_
- These processes are for when the opponent is a human or a similar race. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09007))_
- For example, you probably won't be able to knock a fourlegged monster prone even if you aim for its legs. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09007))_
- For example, you probably won't be able to knock a fourlegged monster prone even if you aim for its legs. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09007))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09001))_

> In this case, you cannot deal damage to your opponent's life force.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09006))_

> Subtract the defender's adventurer level from the result in 2. This final result is the penalty for the success roll.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09007))_

> If an area aim is declared against other monsters, the game master should refer to these and use common sense when making decisions.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09007))_

> These processes are for when the opponent is a human or a similar race. If an area aim is declared against other monsters, the game master should refer to these and use common sense when making decisions. For example, you probably won't be able to knock a fourlegged monster prone even if you aim for its legs. Aiming for a weapon will be in vain against monsters that attack with fangs and claws on their bodies.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09008))_

## 16.6

## Holding Weapons in Both Hands

- In the basic rules, characters cannot hold separate weapons in each hand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09012))_
- This section presents extended rules for holding weapons in both hands . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09012))_
- However, there are many people who admire dual wielding. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09012))_

## 16.6.1 Character's Dominant

## Hand

- Characters are generally righthanded or left-handed, but in actuality there are also people who are ambidextrous . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09015))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09015))_

> Therefore, when creating an adventurer, let's say that if you roll 2D and get double sixes , they'll be ambidextrous.

## 16.7

- Left-handed or right-handedness is decided by the player's preference. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09017))_

## 16.6.2 Two-Handed Attacks

- If a character has weapons in both hands, he or she has three options for attacking. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09019))_
- There's no need to reduce one to a quarter. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09022))_
- Also, the required strength for one of the weapons is limited to half your own strength (rounded up) . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09022))_
- Also, the required strength for one of the weapons is limited to half your own strength (rounded up) . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09022))_
- If you attack with only one hand, there is no penalty, and if you attack with both hands, the penalty is only -2 for each. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09023))_
- When a character is ambidextrous, there is no opposite hand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09023))_
- If you attack with only one hand, there is no penalty, and if you attack with both hands, the penalty is only -2 for each. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09023))_

> your = -2 penalty to attack power.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09022))_

> If you're fighting with the thief skill, the required strength for both weapons is only half the character's strength.

## Throwing Multiple Darts or Daggers

- - All objects thrown at the same time must have the same weight (required strength). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09027))_
- - The total required strength must be up to the character's strength (or half that if it's the thief skill). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09028))_
- - The number thrown at the same time becomes a penalty to attack power. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09030))_
- This optional rule increases the chance that a high-level character will defeat a low-level character or monster in one blow. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09031))_
- There must always be one target. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09032))_
- Never forget the limitation of projectiles: they cannot be used while in an engagement . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09033))_
- Never forget the limitation of projectiles: they cannot be used while in an engagement . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09033))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09030))_

> The number thrown at the same time becomes a penalty to attack power. For example, if you throw three at the same time, that'll incur a -3 penalty to your attack power for each hit check.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09032))_

> - You cannot choose multiple targets.

## Weapons and Armor That Are Too Heavy

- However, at this time, you'll suffer a penalty to your attack power or evasion speed . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09036))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09036))_

> A Character can also use weapons and armor whose required strength exceeds his or her own strength.

## If a Weapon or Shield's Required Strength Exceeds Your Strength

- If one in one hand is too heavy, you will receive a penalty, but there will be no stacking. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09038))_
- For example, if your sword is 3 too heavy in required strength and your shield is 2 too heavy in required strength, your penalty is -3 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09038))_
- For each point of required strength, reduce attack power by -1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09038))_
- For example, if your sword is 3 too heavy in required strength and your shield is 2 too heavy in required strength, your penalty is -3 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09038))_

## If Armor's Required Strength Exceeds Your Strength

- At the game master's decision, any success roll that uses agility bonus as a baseline score may be subject to an equal penalty _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09040))_
- For each point of required strength, reduce evasion speed by 1. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09040))_

## 16.9

## Special Weapons

## Pole Weapons

- A pole weapon is a general term for a two-handed weapon that has a long handle with a metal blade attached to the end. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09046))_
- There are halberds, pole axes, lancers, partisans, glaives, pikes...... _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09046))_
- If he or she fails, they will be swept off their feet and fall prone . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09047))_
- This attack cannot target anything that's not bipedal, and it cannot knock anything larger than the adventurer, such as an ogre, giant, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09047))_
- This attack cannot target anything that's not bipedal, and it cannot knock anything larger than the adventurer, such as an ogre, giant, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09047))_
- Pole weapons have the same basic idea of attacking the enemy from a distance, but their functions are specialized in various ways. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09048))_
- The ways to use these pole weapons are as follows. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09048))_

## Thrust

- Its tip is sharply pointed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09050))_
- Please use spear/2H for attack power modifiers, strike power modifiers, critical target, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09050))_
- Y ou can treat these the same as spears. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09050))_

## Cut

- It has an ax-like blade. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09052))_

## Stab

- Its scores and handling are based on warhammer/2H . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09054))_

## Beat

- It has a hammer-like tip. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09056))_
- Please use the same scores and handling as fl ail/2H . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09056))_
- It cannot be used to declare mercy . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09056))_

## Sweep

- It allows you to sweep your opponent by hooking their foot. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09058))_
- The strike power itself is 0-- this attack method is not suitable for dealing damage), and there are no criticals. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09058))_
- If the attack is successful, the opponent suffers damage and at the same time must make a success roll using adventurer level + dexterity bonus ( evasion points -7 for monsters) as the baseline score and the attacker's final Players should examine which attack methods they can use with each pole weapon (for example, a halberd can be used in four ways: thrust , cut , stab , and sweep ) and arm themselves accordingly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09059))_
- If the attack is successful, the opponent suffers damage and at the same time must make a success roll using adventurer level + dexterity bonus ( evasion points -7 for monsters) as the baseline score and the attacker's final Players should examine which attack methods they can use with each pole weapon (for example, a halberd can be used in four ways: thrust , cut , stab , and sweep ) and arm themselves accordingly. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09059))_
- Below are some typical pole weapons and how to use them. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09060))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09059))_

> For those with multiple attack methods, you must declare which attack method you will use each round.

## Pole Ax

- It can be used in two ways: thrust and cut . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09062))_

## Halberd

- It's the most sophisticated pole weapon, and can be used to thrust , cut , stab , and sweep . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09064))_

## Lucerne Hammer

- On the other side, there's a hookshaped protrusion. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09066))_
- The lucerne hammer has a similar shape to a halberd, but the tip of the spear is longer than a halberd, and the striking part is a hammer rather than an ax. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09066))_
- This weapon can be used to thrust , cut and beat . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09066))_

## Glaive

- The glaive is a weapon with a knifelike blade attached to the end of a long handle. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09068))_
- This weapon can be used in two ways: thrust and cut . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09068))_

## Bill

- The length of a pole weapon is required strength x 15 centimeters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09071))_
- It would not be a good idea to bring them into underground labyrinths, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09071))_
- Pole weapons cannot reach their full power in tight spaces. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09071))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09071))_

> If you don't have a space with this radius, you cannot use attack methods other than thrust .

## Main-gauche/Sword Breaker

- Y ou'll gain a +1 bonus to evasion speed only if your opponent's weapon is a dagger , rapier , or short sword . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09073))_
- A main-gauche is a dagger held in the left hand (right hand for left-handed adventurers) for parrying. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09073))_
- Y ou'll gain a +1 bonus to evasion speed only if your opponent's weapon is a dagger , rapier , or short sword . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09073))_
- It has a similar bonus to evasion speed, and moreover, if used skillfully, can break or drop an opponent's weapon. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09074))_
- If you roll an 11 , and you evade your opponent's attack, you can make your opponent drop his or her weapon. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09074))_
- A sword breaker is a type of maingauche with serrated blades lined up along the back. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09074))_
- Of course, your opponent's weapons are limited to daggers , rapiers , and short swords . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09074))_
- Of course, your opponent's weapons are limited to daggers , rapiers , and short swords . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09074))_
- Main-gauche and sword breaker have the same attack scores as a dagger . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09075))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09074))_

> When you roll 2D for evasion, if you get double sixes , you can break your opponent's weapon.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09074))_

> A sword breaker is a type of maingauche with serrated blades lined up along the back. It has a similar bonus to evasion speed, and moreover, if used skillfully, can break or drop an opponent's weapon. When you roll 2D for evasion, if you get double sixes , you can break your opponent's weapon. If you roll an 11 , and you evade your opponent's attack, you can make your opponent drop his or her weapon. Of course, your opponent's weapons are limited to daggers , rapiers , and short swords .

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09075))_

> Also, if you use it for an attack, you'll lose the evasion bonus.

## Whip, Net, Bola

- If an attack with one of these weapons hits, the evader must make a success roll using adventurer level + agility bonus as the baseline score and attacker's final score at this time as the target score. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09077))_
- If he or she fails, they'll be entangled and fall prone . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09078))_
- Once entangled with an opponent, the weapon is no longer at the attacker's disposal. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09079))_
- To escape, you must first release any armaments you're holding. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09080))_
- While entangled, there is always a -4 penalty evading your opponent's attacks. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09080))_
- If the entangled target has hands or similar organs, the target score will decrease by 1 each round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09080))_
- Once entangled, an escape check is required every round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09080))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09077))_

> These weapons can perform attacks that entangle opponents.

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09077))_

> These weapons can perform attacks that entangle opponents. If an attack with one of these weapons hits, the evader must make a success roll using adventurer level + agility bonus as the baseline score and attacker's final score at this time as the target score.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09079))_

> If the opponent is of a larger race than him or her, the attacker must release the whip or net.

## Whip

- If the attack hits and the opponent is not entangled, the whip will return to the attacker's hand. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09082))_
- At this time, roll 2D, and it'll only entangle the opponent if you roll double ones . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09082))_

- However, its range is limited to 5 meters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09084))_

## Lance

- A lance is a long weapon used by cavalry. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09099))_
- It must be used on horseback and can only be used in a charge (lance charge) . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09099))_
- It must be used on horseback and can only be used in a charge (lance charge) . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09099))_
- During this time, any attacks made against the horse or mounted adventurer must be evaded with a -4 penalty. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09100))_
- Also, even if the horse is a war horse, you cannot make the horse itself attack. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09100))_
- If you both have lances, or if your opponent is using a spear or a pike , follow the rules for charging each other. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09103))_
- However, keep in mind that there is a possibility that you'll face a counterattack from your opponent each time you use a lance charge. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09104))_
- In the case of lance charge, it is also possible to run straight through without entering an engagement. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09104))_

## Sap (Blackjack)

- This weapon has the potential to cause fae, youma, or animals of human size or smaller to lose consciousness when you attack them unnoticed from behind. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09113))_
- If he or she fails, they'll fall unconscious and will not wake up for 2D x 10 minutes . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09114))_
- Normal damage is dealt regardless of success or failure. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09114))_
- The target score is the final score from when the attack hit . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09114))_
- The attacked opponent gains a +4 bonus to his or her resistance roll if he or she is wearing metal armor. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09115))_

## 17.1

## Changes to Magic Procedure

- However, in this case, the master's work becomes quite complicated. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09127))_
- You can unify the rules of magic, allowing for a more integrated approach to casting. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09127))_
- Using that as the target score, everyone on the side on which it was cast must attempt a mental power resistance roll. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09128))_
- If they fail, the magic will be cast with its full effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09128))_
- The magic procedure is resolved the same way regardless of whether a monster or an adventurer is casting the spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09128))_
- In other words, you roll 2D and add it to your magic power . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09128))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09128))_

> If they succeed, the magic will not be cast or its effect will be reduced .

## 17.2

- the caster rolls double ones , the magic will fail completely and have no effect. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09130))_

## Monster Magic Power

- Monster magic power predetermined for each monster. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09132))_

## Monster Resistance

- However, a monster's resistance roll fails if there's a tie . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09135))_
- The baseline score for a monster's mental power resistance roll is resistance points -7 (written in parentheses) . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09135))_
- This is an exception to the rule in order to maintain numerical balance. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09135))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09135))_

> The final score must exceed the final score from when the character cast the spell.

## Lost Spells

- These are called lost spells . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09138))_
- Not all ancient magic spells are taught in the sorcerers' guild. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09138))_
- There are spells known only to a limited number of sorcerers, and also spells that were lost with the fall of the ancient kingdom. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09138))_
- Of course, no matter how high-level a sorcerer is, they don't know every spell. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09138))_
- There are spells known only to a limited number of sorcerers, and also spells that were lost with the fall of the ancient kingdom. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09138))_
- You must know of a spell's existence and learn how to use it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09139))_
- Lost spells cannot be used simply by increasing your sorcerer skill level. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09139))_
- The learning methods are as follows. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09140))_

## 1. Obtain Spellbooks or Scrolls Containing Lost Spell

- Spellbooks and scrolls containing lost spells can be found as treasure in the ruins of the ancient kingdom, and by studying them, you can learn lost spells. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09142))_

## 2. Learn From a Sorcerer Who Knows Lost Spell

- Y ou can learn lost spells by learning from these sorcerers. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09144))_
- There are some sorcerers who, as a result of years of hard work or through sheer luck, have acquired lost spells. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09144))_
- If your skill level is insufficient, you must wait until your skill level increases before you can use it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09145))_
- If your skill level is insufficient, you must wait until your skill level increases before you can use it. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09145))_
- Which spells are considered lost is entirely up to the game master's choice. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09146))_
- As a guideline, Table 17-1: Lost Spells is included, and the individual detail section of those spells also says lost under their type. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09146))_
- However, if the game master wishes, spells not on the list may be designated as lost spells , and some or even all of the spells on the list may be designated as general spells . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09146))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09145))_

> However, even if you learn a lost spell, you cannot use it unless your sorcerer skill is of the spell's level or greater.

## Example:

- A skeleton's resistance score due to their mental power is 9 (2), so the baseline score for the resistance roll is 2 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09148))_
- His magic power is 4. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09148))_
- A shaman is attacked by three skeletons. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09148))_
- A monster will fail to resist if the final scores are tied , so a skeleton needs 11 or greater to resist. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09148))_
- The target score is 8 + 4 = 12 , so the skeletons attempt to resist. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09148))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09148))_

> If the game master rolls the dice three times and gets a 4, a 12, and an 8, then only the second skeleton will be able to reduce the effect of the Fire Bolt .

- magic can be used without a staff, but it cannot be handled effectively . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09161))_

## Full Resistance

- When a character anticipates danger or wants to resist magic cast by an opponent at all costs, they may be able to take the full resistance action during the round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09165))_
- Character movement is limited to within 3 meters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09166))_
- Character movement is limited to within 3 meters. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09166))_

- Also, the character's movement method can only be stand still . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09168))_

- You must be fully aware of the enemy, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09169))_

- This is because these are often ill-suited for the complex movements required by ancient magic spells. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09169))_

## 18.1

## Rules for Awarding Greater Experience Points

- As your character's level increases and you take on more difficult adventures, you may find that 1000 experience points for completing a mission is not enough. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09180))_
- 1000 experience points each is a good score for enjoying Sword World RPG for a long time, but some people may want to reach higher levels more easily. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09180))_
- In such cases, you can increase the experience points for accomplishing missions as follows. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09181))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09181))_

> In such cases, you can increase the experience points for accomplishing missions as follows.

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09182))_

> First, the game master must determine what are the most severe obstacles to accomplishing the mission.

## 18.2

- If it's a monster, this is its monster level , and if it's a trap, it's its difficulty . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09184))_
- Typically, it'll probably be the strongest monster, but in some cases it may be a high difficulty trap. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09184))_
- For example, in a scenario where you negotiate with a dragon, which is a 10th level monster, and get it to give you an item, you don't have to fi ght the dragon and you don't need to roll any dice. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09185))_
- This most severe obstacle has the condition that it must be resolved by rolling the dice, regardless of the character's efforts . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09185))_
- For example, in a scenario where you negotiate with a dragon, which is a 10th level monster, and get it to give you an item, you don't have to fi ght the dragon and you don't need to roll any dice. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09185))_
- These are the experience points for accomplishing the mission. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09187))_
- If a 10th level monster is your biggest obstacle, that's 10 x 500 = 5000 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09187))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09185))_

## Guidelines for Adventurer Age and Ability Score Changes

- On the other hand, when you're a child, you don't have that much ability. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09191))_
- Normally, adventurers are at an age when they can fully demonstrate their ability, from just after adulthood to the middle of their lives, but they may be aged or made younger unnaturally by magic, poison, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09191))_
- Normally, adventurers are at an age when they can fully demonstrate their ability, from just after adulthood to the middle of their lives, but they may be aged or made younger unnaturally by magic, poison, etc. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09191))_
- Here, we provide guidelines regarding the ability scores of characters of such unique ages . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09192))_
- Some may mature early and quickly develop a fine physique. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09193))_
- In the case of humans, assume the ability scores of prime age (from around 15~50 years old) to be 1, and the ability scores of each age are as shown in Table 18-1: Age-Based Ability Score Modification Table. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09193))_
- This is just a guideline, and game masters may set individual differences. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09193))_
- In the case of non-human races, they are considered equal to humans until they reach adulthood. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09195))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09191))_

> Also, if you're a game master, you may need to prepare an old man or a young boy for an NPC.

## Elves

- Aging is not considered. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09198))_

## Dwarves, Grassrunners, Half-Elves

- Aging begins at the age of 100. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09200))_

## 18.3

## Rules for Destroying Structures

- However, if you specifically want to handle this numerically, please use the following rules. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09204))_
- Basically, characters cannot destroy walls or doors, but if you really want to implement something like this, it's fine for the game master to make decisions using his or her own common sense ('You have an ax, so you should be able to break down a wooden door'). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09204))_

## 18.3.1 How to Read the Structure Strength Table

## [ Paper ]

- When its defense points reach 0, its destruction points begin to decrease, and on the next round when destruction points reach 1, the paper object will burn out. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09213))_
- Also, if exposed to fire, it'll ignite immediately, and its defense points will automatically decrease by 1 point every round. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09213))_

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09207))_

> Defense points = 0~3

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09208))_

> Destruction points = 1~10

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09209))_

> Complete immunity = Poison Gas-Type magic

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09210))_

> No Criticals = Bow, crossbow, cold-type magic

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09211))_

> Normal = Swords, spears, axes, maces, flails, slings, rocks, war hammers, barehanded, stone-type, wind cuttertype, blade-type magic

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09212))_

> Ignore Defense Points = Fire-type, electrictype, explosion-type, pure energy-type magic

**Atom:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09213))_

> Special = If you cast " Fire Weapon " magic, strike power will become +20, and even weapons that cannot cause criticals will now be able to cause criticals.

## Defense Points

- There is a range, but the game master should decide on a specific score taking into consideration the thickness. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09215))_

## Destruction Points

- The lowest of this score is called the minimum destruction points . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09217))_
- In the case of paper, the score is 1~10, so the minimum destruction points are 1 . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09217))_
- The lowest of this score is called the minimum destruction points . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09217))_
- Defense points and destruction points are used in the basic system for destroying structures. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09218))_
- However, the structure itself is not destroyed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09219))_
- However, this will only be reduced to the minimum destruction points depending on the material. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09219))_
- After the defense points fall to 0, the destruction points will decrease by 1 point. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09219))_
- If the result is greater than or equal to the destruction points , the structure is destroyed by that blow. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09219))_
- damage is calculated in the same way as when an attack hits a monster. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09219))_
- If the result is 0 or less (when damage is less than or equal to defense points), that means there was no effect at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09219))_
- Then, you subtract the defense points from the determined damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09219))_
- After the defense points fall to 0, the destruction points will decrease by 1 point. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09219))_
- However, this will only be reduced to the minimum destruction points depending on the material. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09219))_
- If the result is 0 or less (when damage is less than or equal to defense points), that means there was no effect at all. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09219))_
- Then, you subtract the defense points from the determined damage. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09219))_
- damage than these minimum destruction points with a single blow , you will not be able to destroy a structure with 0 defense points. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09221))_

**Context:** _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09219))_

> When a character attempts to destroy something with a weapon, magic, etc. damage is calculated in the same way as when an attack hits a monster. Then, you subtract the defense points from the determined damage. If the result is greater than or equal to the destruction points , the structure is destroyed by that blow. ## Example:

- The game master considers this to have maximum strength for a wooden wall, and sets its defense points and destruction points to their maximums of 7 and 15 , respectively (see details below). _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09223))_
- If the damage is 7 points or less , the character's efforts are completely wasted . _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09224))_
- Once the destruction points reach 5 points , they will no longer decrease.) _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09224))_
- If a character attacks with a weapon and deals 22 or greater points of damage in one blow, this wooden wall will be destroyed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09224))_

- If you try this in a closed room, there's a great possibility your character will succumb to the heat before the wall is destroyed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09268))_
- If your character accidentally touches it, he or she will suffer severe burns. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09268))_

## 18.3.3 Handling Spirit Walls

- When using these rules for destroying structures to destroy the spirit magic Spirit Wall, please do so as follows. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09286))_

## [ Spirit Wall: Gnome ]

- Without expansion, defense points are 15 and destruction points are 20. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09288))_
- Its minimum destruction points are 1 because it's stone. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09288))_
- Its minimum destruction points are 1 because it's stone. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09288))_

## [ Spirit Wall: Frau ]

- Without expansion, defense points are 15 and destruction points are 20. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09290))_
- Its minimum destruction points are 1 because it's ice. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09290))_
- Its minimum destruction points are 1 because it's ice. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09290))_

## [ Spirit Wall: Dryad ]

- Without expansion, defense points and destruction points are both 15. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09292))_
- Spirit Wall: Dryad has the power to regenerate even if it's destroyed. _(Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf (source-range-de1806a5-09293))_
